urn:contentItem:6JGY-C4P3-S0P5-J0F4-00000-00ZACHRY GROUP - THE RECENT NEWS INCLUDING PEER NEWS2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:09:17ZLexisNexisSTOCKZACHRY GROUP - THE RECENT NEWS INCLUDING PEER NEWS

DASHBOARD: ZACHRY GROUP

Websitehttps://www.zachrygroup.com/IndustryConstrustion

ACTIVITIES:

Zachry Group, founded in 1924, is a leading construction and engineering firm headquartered in San Antonio, Texas. Employing over 20,000 people, it provides turnkey services in power, energy, chemicals, and industrial sectors, generating over $3 billion annually. Its subsidiaries, like JVIC, offer maintenance and fabrication. In 2024, Zachry expanded renewable energy projects, including solar and wind facilities. Its culture emphasizes safety, citizenship, and employee training, with a commitment to community health and ethical practices. Competing with Fluor, Zachry stands out for its EPC (engineering, procurement, construction) expertise and safety standards. Recent initiatives include digital project management tools and carbon-neutral construction goals, aligning with sustainability trends.

+ ZACHRY GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ ZACHRY GROUP EXPANDS FOOTPRINT WITH ROOSEVELT BRIDGE PROJECT IN OKLAHOMA

Zachry Construction Corporation is making its entry into the Oklahoma market by securing a contract for the US-70 Roosevelt Bridge replacement project. The Oklahoma Department of Transportation selected the Zachry Group in a joint venture with Traylor Bros., Inc. to deliver this significant infrastructure upgrade. This partnership marks the first collaboration between Zachry Construction and the Oklahoma DOT, highlighting the company's ambition to expand its regional presence.The announcement, made on 28th April 2026, signifies a major investment in modernizing a critical transportation corridor that connects Bryan and Marshall counties. The existing Roosevelt Bridge has been operational since 1945 and serves as an essential link for local communities, businesses, and visitors traveling to the Lake Texoma area. With the new bridge, Zachry Construction aims to enhance mobility, safety, and economic activity in southeastern Oklahoma.Zachry and Traylor Bros. will construct a new four-lane bridge that includes modern features such as bicycle and pedestrian accommodations, a median barrier, and upgraded lighting. The project also entails the demolition of the existing bridge, with traffic being maintained on it during the construction phase to minimize disruption to the traveling public. Echoing the significance of this venture, Travis Mross, president of Zachry Construction, stated, 'This project is an important milestone for Zachry, as it strengthens a vital connection for the communities around Lake Texoma.'The project is designed to improve safety, reliability, and resilience for the future. It aims to address the increasing traffic demands in the area while ensuring that local commerce and tourism continue to flourish. The Oklahoma Department of Transportation has set a completion target of 1st October 2029 for the Roosevelt Bridge replacement project, which is expected to elevate the standard of infrastructure in the region.Zachry Construction Corporation, a third-generation family business with over a century of experience, will leverage its extensive expertise in heavy and highway construction for this initiative. The joint venture with Traylor Bros., known for its proficiency in complex bridge projects, promises to deliver a state-of-the-art crossing that enhances connectivity and maintains mobility throughout construction. Together, they are poised to provide lasting value to the state and its communities.

+ ZACHRY CONSTRUCTION CORP. ELEVATES MROSS TO PRESIDENT AMID STRATEGIC GROWTH

Zachry Construction Corp. has appointed Travis Mross as its new president, marking a significant transition in leadership for the San Antonio-based company. Mross, who began his career with the family-owned business as an intern in 2000, officially took on the presidential role on 29th April 2026.Over the years, Mross has steadily advanced through the ranks, culminating in his previous position as executive vice president for the past six years. In this role, he played a crucial part in shaping Zachry Construction's strategic direction and enhancing operational efficiency. He succeeds David Zachry, who will now concentrate solely on his responsibilities as CEO."Travis has been instrumental in setting our course and building the strong trajectory we are on today," David Zachry stated. He emphasized Mross's deep understanding of the business and his contributions to instilling discipline and accountability, which are essential for the company's continued success. Under Mross's leadership, Zachry Construction aims to maintain its momentum and explore new opportunities.Zachry Construction Corp. is recognized for its extensive work in heavy civil, roadway, and infrastructure projects, with over 6,000 completed initiatives in both public and private sectors. The company is currently expanding its operations in the water and wastewater infrastructure domain, considered one of the fastest-growing segments in the construction industry. Recent projects include water storage and dams for various authorities, and the acquisition of Crescent Constructors Inc. has further strengthened its capabilities in this area.As president, Mross will oversee operations across multiple sectors, including water, transportation, underground utilities, and civil sitework. His priorities will focus on strategic growth, operational excellence, and nurturing a robust corporate culture while maintaining a gritty mindset. Mross stated, "Our job is to bring consistency, discipline, and a high standard of execution to every project, which will guide how we serve clients and grow the business."

+ ZACHRY GROUP EXPANDS FOOTPRINT WITH NEW LEASE NEAR LA VILLITA

Zachry Group has recently taken over a ground lease for a parking lot near La Villita and Hemisfair, marking a strategic move to support its operations in the area. On the 29th of April 2026, the company, through its affiliate Zachry Park Garage QOZB LLC, finalized the lease agreement with St. John’s Evangelical Lutheran Church, acquiring the rights to a parking lot situated across South Presa Street from the church.In addition to the lease, Zachry Group also purchased a smaller parking lot from St. Johns Four Square LLC, bringing the total area acquired to approximately 1.2 acres. Both properties were previously associated with Dennis McDaniel, an Austin developer who had intended to build an apartment complex on the site before the onset of the pandemic disrupted those plans.According to a representative from Zachry Group, the company intends to utilize the parking lots for operational purposes related to the luxury Monarch San Antonio hotel, which recently opened its doors overlooking Civic Park. There are currently no plans for further development on the newly acquired properties, indicating a focus on supporting existing hospitality operations rather than pursuing new construction projects.The properties were originally part of a larger development plan initiated by McDaniel in partnership with Opportunity Home San Antonio, the city's public housing authority. This ambitious project, which was to feature 252 apartments and retail space, fell through as Opportunity Home stepped back during the pandemic, leaving the construction plans unfulfilled.Zachry Group, a prominent player in various sectors including construction and hospitality, has a long-standing history in San Antonio. Founded in 1924 by Henry Bartell 'Pat' Zachry, the company has contributed significantly to local developments, including the recent $185 million Monarch hotel project. With its roots deeply entrenched in the city’s growth, Zachry Group continues to play a vital role in shaping San Antonio's urban landscape.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BECHTEL [3 News That Matters]

+ BECHTEL PARTNERS WITH SOWELA TO ENHANCE PIPEFITTING SKILLS IN LAKE CHARLES

Bechtel, a leading global engineering and construction firm, has announced a partnership with SOWELA Technical Community College to introduce a free Pipefitting Training Program at SOWELA’s Main Campus in Lake Charles. This initiative aims to equip local students with the essential skills needed for a career in pipefitting, a critical trade in various industries including manufacturing and power generation. The program is set to commence on 26th May 2026 and will run until 13th November 2026.The training program is designed to provide students with a comprehensive introduction to the pipefitting trade, covering core job responsibilities, safety principles, and the tools and materials commonly used in the field. Participants will engage in hands-on learning, focusing on the installation and repair of high- and low-pressure pipe systems, which are vital to the functioning of manufacturing facilities, power generation plants, and building heating and cooling systems.Classes will be held Monday through Friday, from 8 a.m. to 3 p.m., allowing participants to gain a thorough understanding of the pipefitting profession over the course of the program. Upon successful completion, students will earn an OSHA 10-hour safety credential and will be well-prepared to pursue the National Center for Construction Education and Research (NCCER) Pipefitting certifications, enhancing their employability in a competitive job market.To qualify for employment consideration, successful program completers must pass a background check and drug screening, ensuring that they meet industry standards. Interested individuals can find more information or register for the program by visiting www.sowela.edu/pipefitting. For further inquiries, SOWELA’s Office of Workforce Solutions can be contacted at (337) 421-6560 or via email at workforce@sowela.edu.This collaboration between Bechtel and SOWELA highlights the commitment of both organizations to workforce development in the Lake Charles area, addressing the growing demand for skilled labor in the construction and manufacturing sectors. By providing accessible training opportunities, the program aims to empower local residents and contribute to the region 's economic growth.

+ BECHTEL-ENKA CONSORTIUM UNDER PRESSURE TO MEET HIGHWAY DEADLINES IN SERBIA

Serbian President Aleksandar Vu i announced today that he met with representatives of the Bechtel-Enka company, discussing the ongoing construction of a highway that aims to connect multiple cities across Serbia. During this meeting, held on the 21st of April 2026, Vu i emphasized the importance of adhering to the project timelines, particularly for the section stretching from a ak to Kraljevo.Vu i stated, "I am particularly adamant about respecting the deadlines in the a ak-Kraljevo section, which holds significant importance for all of western and southwestern Serbia." This section is part of a larger initiative, the Moravian Corridor, that is seen as one of the most complex infrastructural projects in Serbia's recent history. The government views the timely completion of this highway as crucial for regional development and connectivity.The Bechtel-Enka consortium has faced scrutiny in the past regarding project timelines and budget overruns in various international projects. As Serbia continues to invest heavily in infrastructure development, the pressure is mounting on Bechtel-Enka to deliver as promised. Vu i 's insistence on meeting deadlines reflects the government's broader strategy to enhance economic growth through improved transportation networks.This highway project not only aims to facilitate domestic travel and commerce but also seeks to attract foreign investment by improving Serbia's infrastructure. The government believes that a well-developed transport network will contribute positively to the overall economic landscape of the country, making timely project completion all the more critical.As the Bechtel-Enka consortium moves forward with the construction, stakeholders will be closely monitoring progress to ensure that the timelines set by Vu i are met. The outcome of this project could have significant implications for both the consortium and the Serbian economy as a whole.

+ BECHTEL'S CRAIG ALBERT FUELS NEW CONSTRUCTION PROGRAM AT UNC CHARLOTTE

UNC Charlotte alumni Craig and Darla Albert have made a significant leadership gift to the university, aimed at launching a new model for construction education in one of the nation’s fastest-growing markets. This initiative will be housed within The Albert School of Construction in the William States Lee College of Engineering. The school will emphasize the full capital project lifecycle, preparing students to lead the planning, engineering, and delivery of complex infrastructure projects. This announcement comes on the 7th of April, 2026.Chancellor Sharon L. Gaber highlighted the importance of this investment, stating, "This investment allows us to align education more directly with the needs of our region and the nation." As Charlotte and North Carolina continue to grow, the complexity of infrastructure demands increases, necessitating graduates who are equipped to contribute effectively. Craig Albert, the president and chief operating officer of Bechtel Group, emphasized the pressing need for a skilled workforce to support essential industries, noting a real shortage of construction capacity.The Albert School of Construction aims to build upon the university’s 50 years of construction education by integrating disciplines across engineering, business, and data science into a cohesive academic framework. Rob Keynton, dean of the William States Lee College of Engineering, explained that the program’s unique approach encompasses the entire capital project lifecycle. This comprehensive perspective positions UNC Charlotte as a leader in the evolution of construction education, emphasizing industry partnerships and research strengths.Leading the school will be Executive Director Lingguang Song, who has a background as a professor and department chair in engineering technology and construction management. By offering a curriculum that aligns closely with industry needs, the program aspires to ensure graduates can adapt quickly to professional demands. Craig Albert stated, "My hope is that companies will see graduates from this program climbing the learning curve more quickly. They’ll be equipped for exactly what the industry needs."The Alberts’ leadership gift marks the initial phase of a broader initiative to expand the school’s facilities, programs, and industry collaborations. Future plans include the development of hands-on learning environments, an integrated curriculum with industry input, and endowed faculty positions in critical construction areas. The Alberts are also co-chairs of the For the Love of Charlotte campaign, initiated to raise a record $500 million for the university. Craig Albert expressed a desire for other successful alumni to follow their lead in supporting UNC Charlotte’s growth and impact.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION PARTNERS IN $7.1 BILLION AUSTIN LIGHT RAIL PROJECT

A joint venture between Kiewit Building Group and Austin Commercial has secured a significant design-build contract to construct an operations and maintenance facility for the US$7.1 billion Austin Light Rail line. This announcement was made on 28th April 2026, marking a pivotal moment in the development of public transit infrastructure in Austin, Texas.The planned railway system will encompass 15 stations along a 10-mile stretch, featuring all-electric trains expected to run every five to ten minutes throughout most of the day. Kiewit Corporation, in collaboration with local partner Austin Commercial, will oversee the design and construction of the facility where Austin’s light rail vehicles will be stored, serviced, and dispatched, ensuring operational efficiency.In addition to storage and maintenance, the facility will provide a workspace for operators, maintenance crews, and support staff, thereby contributing to the overall functionality of the light rail system. The selected team for this project includes 10 subcontractors focused on the design phase, which is anticipated to generate thousands of jobs during both the design and construction phases.Greg Canally, CEO of the Austin Transit Partnership (ATP), emphasized the importance of this development, stating, "This is another major step forward, and we now have the full construction team that will deliver Austin’s light rail system. The Operations and Maintenance Facility is integral to the success of the system—it’s where trains are maintained, cleaned and prepared for service each day." Canally highlighted that partnering with a world-class construction firm like Kiewit Corporation, which possesses deep local experience, positions the project for long-term success while fostering job creation and economic opportunities across Central Texas.Previously, an Aecom-led consortium named LINC Austin was awarded the role of delivery partner for the first phase of the larger rail project in December 2024. This consortium includes notable firms such as Parsons, STV, Turner & Townsend, Ardmore Roderick, and CAS Consulting, all of which will play a crucial role in the realization of Austin's light rail ambitions.

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION SELECTED FOR EAST AUSTIN LIGHT RAIL PROJECT

A major piece of Austin's long-discussed light rail is finally moving from plans to prep work. On Wednesday, the Austin Transit Partnership signed off on Kiewit Austin Partnership as the design-build contractor for the light rail Operations and Maintenance Facility, clearing the way for pre-construction and inching a central Project Connect component closer to groundbreakers and bulldozers near the airport. The date of this significant development is 21st April 2026.According to Austin Transit Partnership, the ATP board approved Kiewit Austin Partnership—a joint venture of Kiewit Building Group Inc. and Austin Commercial—as the design-build contractor for the Operations and Maintenance Facility. ATP CEO Greg Canally remarked, “This is another major step forward, and we now have the full construction team that will deliver Austin’s light rail system.” The agency's decision allows KAP to immediately launch pre-construction work, including advancing design, starting the permitting process, and preparing the site for future construction.The operations and maintenance facility is slated for a 62-acre site at the southwest edge of the Montopolis neighborhood, according to ATP. The planned footprint, near U.S. Highway 183 and State Highway 71, will house storage and servicing for up to 40 light rail vehicles and will necessitate property acquisitions affecting dozens of existing businesses. Local reporting has highlighted that roughly one-third of the businesses expected to be displaced are concentrated in Airport Commerce Park, raising concerns about relocation and neighborhood impacts.With KAP in place, ATP plans to expedite the Operations and Maintenance Facility design through permitting and site preparation, paving the way for the broader light rail project to advance toward full construction. Industry estimates indicate that mainline construction could commence in 2027, with initial service targeted for the early 2030s. The Federal Transit Administration's combined Final Environmental Impact Statement and Record of Decision, published in January 2026, has cleared the project to enter final design and begin land acquisition.ATP noted that the KAP team already includes around 10 subcontractors during the design phase, with opportunities expected to increase once construction ramps up. The larger Project Connect program is projected to create or support thousands—potentially tens of thousands—of jobs across Texas during design and construction. On the operations side, the maintenance facility is expected to support approximately 300 long-term jobs once the system is operational, further contributing to local economic growth.

+ ATERNIUM ENGAGES KIEWIT FOR HYDROGEN PRODUCTION FACILITY PRE-FEED

Aternium has appointed Kiewit Engineering Group Inc. to undertake the pre-front-end engineering and design (pre-FEED) for its inaugural integrated hydrogen and heavy water production facility. This strategic move comes as Aternium aims to advance its ambitious $1 billion plan to produce clean hydrogen and critical heavy water domestically. The announcement was made on 26th March 2026, signaling a significant step in the company's broader, multi-plant development strategy focused on establishing a robust infrastructure for advanced energy materials.The planned facility will be located in the Mid-Atlantic region of the United States, designed to meet the growing demand for low-carbon energy solutions. Aternium's initiative not only seeks to enhance domestic capabilities in the production of heavy water—currently sourced primarily from countries like China and Iran—but also aims to address critical supply chain vulnerabilities. Andrew Cottone, Founder and CEO of Aternium, emphasized the importance of this collaboration with Kiewit, citing shared values in technical expertise and safety standards.According to Cottone, the initiative is poised to create significant socioeconomic benefits by fostering workforce development and job creation in local communities. Aternium's focus on integrating advanced technologies and innovative processes is expected to stimulate regional economic growth, thereby supporting the transition to cleaner energy systems. Kiewit will play a pivotal role in executing Aternium's unique business model, which aims to deliver both clean hydrogen and heavy water at competitive costs.Kiewit’s involvement in the project brings extensive experience in engineering, procurement, and construction within the energy sector. Tyler Nordquist, Executive Vice President at Kiewit Engineering Group Inc., expressed confidence in the partnership, highlighting the company's ability to add substantial value during the pre-FEED phase. This phase will include defining the project's scope, optimizing design parameters, and laying a solid foundation for future development.Aternium's integrated production approach is noteworthy as it combines clean hydrogen generation with a patent-pending process for isolating heavy water. This dual-output model not only aims to reduce hydrogen production costs but also addresses a critical vulnerability in the U.S. supply of strategic materials. With the increasing demand for clean energy and high-tech components, Aternium's initiative is strategically positioned at the intersection of energy transition and advanced manufacturing, thus offering a scalable solution that aligns with both economic and technological priorities.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION EXPANDS INNSBROOK OFFICE WITH LOCAL SUPPORT

DPR Construction, a leading regional construction firm, is set to expand its office space in Innsbrook by 9,100 square feet. The announcement comes as part of a strategic move to accommodate the company's growing workforce and enhance its operational capabilities. The expansion is supported by local incentives from Henrico County, which aims to attract and retain businesses in the area. The date of this announcement is 29th April 2026.The new office space will allow DPR Construction to bring together teams and resources, fostering collaboration and innovation within the company. This growth is expected to create additional job opportunities in the region, further contributing to Henrico's economic development goals. Local officials have expressed optimism about the project, viewing it as a significant investment in the community's future.DPR Construction has been a prominent player in the construction industry, known for its commitment to quality and sustainability. The company's decision to expand its presence in Innsbrook reflects its confidence in the local market and the potential for continued growth. The support from Henrico County is part of broader efforts to enhance the business environment, making it easier for companies to invest and thrive.As part of the expansion, DPR Construction plans to implement modern design features and sustainable practices in the new office layout. This aligns with the company's mission to lead in sustainable construction practices while also providing a conducive work environment for its employees. The expansion is anticipated to be completed within the next year, further solidifying DPR Construction's commitment to the region.In addition to the expansion, DPR Construction has been actively involved in various community initiatives, underscoring its dedication to corporate social responsibility. As the company prepares for this new chapter, stakeholders are keenly watching how this expansion will impact both the firm and the local economy.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION APPOINTS JASON PAGE TO LEAD NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP

DPR Construction has announced the appointment of Jason Page as the new lead for its Special Services Group in Nashville. As of 1st April 2026, Page brings over 14 years of experience to the role, having joined DPR Construction in January 2012. His career has been marked by a versatile portfolio, encompassing projects from minor renovations to large-scale, complex developments.Since relocating to Nashville in April 2021, Jason Page has successfully spearheaded numerous corporate interior and entertainment projects, consistently delivering high-quality results under tight deadlines. His approach emphasizes a client-first leadership style, which has allowed him to cultivate strong relationships across various market sectors in the region. Page's passion for renovation and tenant improvement work has positioned him as a key player in DPR Construction's local operations.The DPR Special Services Group (SSG) plays a pivotal role in executing a diverse range of projects, from targeted improvements like parking garage stair installations to expansive interior buildouts for new office and entertainment spaces. Under Page's leadership, the SSG is expected to enhance its capabilities in meeting the evolving needs of clients and stakeholders in Nashville.In addition to project leadership, Jason Page has been actively involved in mentoring and coaching project teams within DPR Construction. He has shared best practices for executing quick-turn, high-impact work, fostering a culture of collaboration and innovation. His dedication to developing talent within the organization is seen as a valuable asset as DPR Construction continues to expand its footprint in Nashville.With Jason Page at the helm of the Special Services Group, DPR Construction is well-positioned to leverage his expertise and leadership as it navigates the competitive landscape of Nashville's construction market. The company's commitment to delivering exceptional service and quality projects remains steadfast, supported by Page's extensive experience and strategic vision.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ACHIEVES GREAT PLACE TO WORK® CERTIFICATION AMID HIGH EMPLOYEE SATISFACTION

DPR Construction, a leader in the technical building sector, has secured Great Place To Work® Certification, with an impressive 97 percent of its employees affirming that it is a great place to work. This remarkable statistic is notably 40 points higher than the typical score given to workplaces across the United States. The certification, awarded on 31st March 2026, underscores DPR's commitment to fostering a workplace culture characterized by pride, belonging, and opportunity.George Pfeffer, CEO of DPR Construction, emphasized the significance of this certification, which reflects the sentiments of the employees who embody the company's culture. He stated, "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life." Pfeffer highlighted the company's focus on creating an inclusive environment where employees feel trusted, supported, and encouraged to grow, thus enabling them to perform at their best.DPR Construction's operational philosophy is built on empowering employees across its various projects to make decisions and share information openly. This approach encourages initiative, whether it involves innovating on complex tasks, stepping into new roles, or contributing ideas beyond formal job descriptions. Employees benefit from career paths aligned with their aspirations, supported by various learning and development programs.The research conducted by Great Place To Work® corroborates DPR's employee-centric strategy, revealing that a significant portion of the workforce feels a sense of pride in their contributions and a strong sense of belonging within the company. The certification further enhances DPR's reputation as an employer of choice, with additional accolades from U.S. News & World Report and Newsweek recognizing it as one of the best companies to work for, particularly in over 15 U.S. cities over the past year.DPR Construction has built a strong legacy since its founding in 1990, evolving into a multi-billion-dollar organization that specializes in complex and sustainable projects across various sectors. Its commitment to innovation, through the use of virtual design and construction and self-performing craft, positions DPR among the top contractors in the industry, employing over 11,000 professionals worldwide. For more details about DPR's culture and career opportunities, visit their official website.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PARSONS CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ PARSONS CORPORATION EXPANDS ROLE IN NEW YORK'S INFRASTRUCTURE DEVELOPMENTS

Parsons Corporation (NYSE:PSN) has been selected to manage the construction of the Newtown Creek Combined Sewer Overflow Storage Tunnel in New York City. This announcement comes on the heels of a significant contract awarded on the 14th of April 2026, which highlights the company's commitment to innovative infrastructure solutions. As part of this project, Parsons Corporation will oversee the development of a large-diameter storage tunnel and a dewatering pump station aimed at protecting local waterways from untreated discharges.The New York City Department of Environmental Protection (DEP) has entrusted Parsons Corporation with this critical task, recognizing the firm's extensive experience in managing over 250 tunnel and ocean outfall projects worldwide. This infrastructure initiative is not only intended to modernize New York City's physical assets but also to enhance public health and improve water quality throughout the region. By leveraging its technology-driven approach, Parsons Corporation aims to deliver effective solutions that are essential for urban sustainability.In addition to its work in New York, Parsons Corporation has further solidified its position in the defense sector. A joint venture with GSI Americas has secured a $25 million contract with the United States Coast Guard (USCG). This agreement focuses on providing program management services that are vital to the USCG’s Execution Plan, which includes significant upgrades to shore infrastructure and the implementation of transformational technologies.The partnership with GSI Americas underscores Parsons Corporation's strategic intent to support federal agencies in securing and modernizing critical digital and physical assets. This multifaceted approach not only enhances the operational capabilities of the USCG but also aligns with the company's broader objectives in the defense and cybersecurity markets.Overall, Parsons Corporation's recent contracts in both infrastructure and defense sectors demonstrate its versatility and commitment to delivering integrated solutions that meet the evolving needs of urban environments and national security. As the firm continues to expand its portfolio, investors may find interest in its potential for future growth and innovation in these key areas.

+ PARSONS TEAMS WITH GSIA FOR $25 MILLION US COAST GUARD CONTRACT

CHANTILLY, Va. — Parsons Corporation (NYSE: PSN) announced today that its joint venture with GSI Americas Inc. (GSIA) has been awarded a contract valued at $25 million by the United States Coast Guard (USCG) for program management services. This contract, granted on 15th April 2026, includes a one-year base period of performance with four additional one-year option periods.The scope of this contract entails providing program management services that will facilitate the USCG’s ambitious Force Design 2028 Execution Plan, which represents the largest capital investment in the history of the Coast Guard. This comprehensive initiative aims to enhance USCG capabilities through significant upgrades to assets, technology, and shore infrastructure, supported by a total budget of $24.5 billion allocated for modernizing platforms and systems, improving training facilities, and boosting mission-critical operational capacities.Martin Boson, president of Engineered Systems for Parsons, expressed enthusiasm for the partnership, stating, "We look forward to leveraging our team’s combined global program management experience and project delivery success to support the U.S. Coast Guard in this historic modernization effort that strengthens readiness and builds a more agile, capable, and responsive force." As a leader in program management, Parsons brings a wealth of expertise and integrated services essential for the successful execution of this significant plan.GSIA, known for its innovative and sustainable energy solutions, will also play a pivotal role in this joint venture. Michael Warminsky, senior vice president for GSIA, commented, "We are proud to support the U.S. Coast Guard’s Force Design 2028 Execution Plan alongside Parsons, contributing to a program that enhances operational readiness, modernizes critical infrastructure, and strengthens the nation’s maritime security posture."With a strong track record in managing complex projects, Parsons is recognized as the top program management services firm by Engineering News-Record. The company provides comprehensive support throughout the project lifecycle, including master planning, design, construction management, and asset management. This partnership with GSIA further underscores Parsons' commitment to delivering impactful solutions in national security and infrastructure.

+ PARSONS CORPORATION WINS $409M ROOSEVELT BRIDGE DESIGN PROJECT

Parsons Corporation (NYSE:PSN) has been selected as the lead designer for the Roosevelt Bridge Replacement Project, a significant infrastructure initiative valued at $409 million. The announcement was made on 14th April 2026, detailing the project’s aim to replace an aging crossing over Lake Texoma in Bryan and Marshall Counties, Oklahoma.The Oklahoma Department of Transportation has awarded the construction contract to the Zachry Construction/Traylor Brothers Construction Joint Venture, while Parsons will handle the design aspects of the new bridge. This structure will span approximately two miles over one of the largest reservoirs in the United States, which is managed by the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers. The design will include 63 spans, with substructure elements extending about 150 feet below the lake surface.To facilitate ongoing traffic flow during construction, the project will be executed in two phases. The original Roosevelt Bridge, constructed in 1945, serves as a vital connector for local residents, freight, and travelers. The replacement initiative has secured Oklahoma’s largest federal grant amounting to $124 million in 2024, complemented by state funding.Despite the recent contract win, Parsons’ stock has experienced a decline of 35% over the past six months, currently trading at $56.01, with a market capitalization of approximately $6 billion. Analysts have indicated that the stock may be undervalued relative to its fair value, positioning it as a potential opportunity for investors. The company’s president of Infrastructure North America, Mark Fialkowski, emphasized Parsons’ legacy in bridge design, stating that this project exemplifies their commitment to delivering complex infrastructure solutions worldwide.In addition to the Roosevelt Bridge project, Parsons has been active in various significant initiatives, including a 10-year contract with the Utah Department of Transportation for a Vehicle-to-Everything Maintenance and Management System. Furthermore, Parsons is leading the design for a $389 million project on Interstate 64 in Virginia and has been appointed as the project management consultant for the Al Ittihad Sports Village in Saudi Arabia. Despite these advancements, Baird recently downgraded Parsons’ stock rating to Neutral, citing concerns over potential revenue declines in the Middle East, illustrating the complex landscape the company navigates in its growth trajectory.

urn:contentItem:6JGH-K913-SCG7-44M5-00000-00ZACHRY GROUP - RECENT NEWS UPDATE FROM BECHTEL CORPORATION AND PEERS2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:09:17ZLexisNexisSTOCKZACHRY GROUP - RECENT NEWS UPDATE FROM BECHTEL CORPORATION AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: ZACHRY GROUP

Websitehttps://www.zachrygroup.com/IndustryConstrustion

ACTIVITIES:

Zachry Group, founded in 1924, is a leading construction and engineering firm headquartered in San Antonio, Texas. Employing over 20,000 people, it provides turnkey services in power, energy, chemicals, and industrial sectors, generating over $3 billion annually. Its subsidiaries, like JVIC, offer maintenance and fabrication. In 2024, Zachry expanded renewable energy projects, including solar and wind facilities. Its culture emphasizes safety, citizenship, and employee training, with a commitment to community health and ethical practices. Competing with Fluor, Zachry stands out for its EPC (engineering, procurement, construction) expertise and safety standards. Recent initiatives include digital project management tools and carbon-neutral construction goals, aligning with sustainability trends.

+ ZACHRY GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ ZACHRY GROUP EXPANDS FOOTPRINT WITH ROOSEVELT BRIDGE PROJECT IN OKLAHOMA

Zachry Construction Corporation is making its entry into the Oklahoma market by securing a contract for the US-70 Roosevelt Bridge replacement project. The Oklahoma Department of Transportation selected the Zachry Group in a joint venture with Traylor Bros., Inc. to deliver this significant infrastructure upgrade. This partnership marks the first collaboration between Zachry Construction and the Oklahoma DOT, highlighting the company's ambition to expand its regional presence.The announcement, made on 28th April 2026, signifies a major investment in modernizing a critical transportation corridor that connects Bryan and Marshall counties. The existing Roosevelt Bridge has been operational since 1945 and serves as an essential link for local communities, businesses, and visitors traveling to the Lake Texoma area. With the new bridge, Zachry Construction aims to enhance mobility, safety, and economic activity in southeastern Oklahoma.Zachry and Traylor Bros. will construct a new four-lane bridge that includes modern features such as bicycle and pedestrian accommodations, a median barrier, and upgraded lighting. The project also entails the demolition of the existing bridge, with traffic being maintained on it during the construction phase to minimize disruption to the traveling public. Echoing the significance of this venture, Travis Mross, president of Zachry Construction, stated, 'This project is an important milestone for Zachry, as it strengthens a vital connection for the communities around Lake Texoma.'The project is designed to improve safety, reliability, and resilience for the future. It aims to address the increasing traffic demands in the area while ensuring that local commerce and tourism continue to flourish. The Oklahoma Department of Transportation has set a completion target of 1st October 2029 for the Roosevelt Bridge replacement project, which is expected to elevate the standard of infrastructure in the region.Zachry Construction Corporation, a third-generation family business with over a century of experience, will leverage its extensive expertise in heavy and highway construction for this initiative. The joint venture with Traylor Bros., known for its proficiency in complex bridge projects, promises to deliver a state-of-the-art crossing that enhances connectivity and maintains mobility throughout construction. Together, they are poised to provide lasting value to the state and its communities.

+ ZACHRY CONSTRUCTION CORP. ELEVATES MROSS TO PRESIDENT AMID STRATEGIC GROWTH

Zachry Construction Corp. has appointed Travis Mross as its new president, marking a significant transition in leadership for the San Antonio-based company. Mross, who began his career with the family-owned business as an intern in 2000, officially took on the presidential role on 29th April 2026.Over the years, Mross has steadily advanced through the ranks, culminating in his previous position as executive vice president for the past six years. In this role, he played a crucial part in shaping Zachry Construction's strategic direction and enhancing operational efficiency. He succeeds David Zachry, who will now concentrate solely on his responsibilities as CEO."Travis has been instrumental in setting our course and building the strong trajectory we are on today," David Zachry stated. He emphasized Mross's deep understanding of the business and his contributions to instilling discipline and accountability, which are essential for the company's continued success. Under Mross's leadership, Zachry Construction aims to maintain its momentum and explore new opportunities.Zachry Construction Corp. is recognized for its extensive work in heavy civil, roadway, and infrastructure projects, with over 6,000 completed initiatives in both public and private sectors. The company is currently expanding its operations in the water and wastewater infrastructure domain, considered one of the fastest-growing segments in the construction industry. Recent projects include water storage and dams for various authorities, and the acquisition of Crescent Constructors Inc. has further strengthened its capabilities in this area.As president, Mross will oversee operations across multiple sectors, including water, transportation, underground utilities, and civil sitework. His priorities will focus on strategic growth, operational excellence, and nurturing a robust corporate culture while maintaining a gritty mindset. Mross stated, "Our job is to bring consistency, discipline, and a high standard of execution to every project, which will guide how we serve clients and grow the business."

+ ZACHRY GROUP EXPANDS FOOTPRINT WITH NEW LEASE NEAR LA VILLITA

Zachry Group has recently taken over a ground lease for a parking lot near La Villita and Hemisfair, marking a strategic move to support its operations in the area. On the 29th of April 2026, the company, through its affiliate Zachry Park Garage QOZB LLC, finalized the lease agreement with St. John’s Evangelical Lutheran Church, acquiring the rights to a parking lot situated across South Presa Street from the church.In addition to the lease, Zachry Group also purchased a smaller parking lot from St. Johns Four Square LLC, bringing the total area acquired to approximately 1.2 acres. Both properties were previously associated with Dennis McDaniel, an Austin developer who had intended to build an apartment complex on the site before the onset of the pandemic disrupted those plans.According to a representative from Zachry Group, the company intends to utilize the parking lots for operational purposes related to the luxury Monarch San Antonio hotel, which recently opened its doors overlooking Civic Park. There are currently no plans for further development on the newly acquired properties, indicating a focus on supporting existing hospitality operations rather than pursuing new construction projects.The properties were originally part of a larger development plan initiated by McDaniel in partnership with Opportunity Home San Antonio, the city's public housing authority. This ambitious project, which was to feature 252 apartments and retail space, fell through as Opportunity Home stepped back during the pandemic, leaving the construction plans unfulfilled.Zachry Group, a prominent player in various sectors including construction and hospitality, has a long-standing history in San Antonio. Founded in 1924 by Henry Bartell 'Pat' Zachry, the company has contributed significantly to local developments, including the recent $185 million Monarch hotel project. With its roots deeply entrenched in the city’s growth, Zachry Group continues to play a vital role in shaping San Antonio's urban landscape.

+ BECHTEL [3 News That Matters]

+ BECHTEL PARTNERS WITH SOWELA TO ENHANCE PIPEFITTING SKILLS IN LAKE CHARLES

Bechtel, a leading global engineering and construction firm, has announced a partnership with SOWELA Technical Community College to introduce a free Pipefitting Training Program at SOWELA’s Main Campus in Lake Charles. This initiative aims to equip local students with the essential skills needed for a career in pipefitting, a critical trade in various industries including manufacturing and power generation. The program is set to commence on 26th May 2026 and will run until 13th November 2026.The training program is designed to provide students with a comprehensive introduction to the pipefitting trade, covering core job responsibilities, safety principles, and the tools and materials commonly used in the field. Participants will engage in hands-on learning, focusing on the installation and repair of high- and low-pressure pipe systems, which are vital to the functioning of manufacturing facilities, power generation plants, and building heating and cooling systems.Classes will be held Monday through Friday, from 8 a.m. to 3 p.m., allowing participants to gain a thorough understanding of the pipefitting profession over the course of the program. Upon successful completion, students will earn an OSHA 10-hour safety credential and will be well-prepared to pursue the National Center for Construction Education and Research (NCCER) Pipefitting certifications, enhancing their employability in a competitive job market.To qualify for employment consideration, successful program completers must pass a background check and drug screening, ensuring that they meet industry standards. Interested individuals can find more information or register for the program by visiting www.sowela.edu/pipefitting. For further inquiries, SOWELA’s Office of Workforce Solutions can be contacted at (337) 421-6560 or via email at workforce@sowela.edu.This collaboration between Bechtel and SOWELA highlights the commitment of both organizations to workforce development in the Lake Charles area, addressing the growing demand for skilled labor in the construction and manufacturing sectors. By providing accessible training opportunities, the program aims to empower local residents and contribute to the region 's economic growth.

+ BECHTEL-ENKA CONSORTIUM UNDER PRESSURE TO MEET HIGHWAY DEADLINES IN SERBIA

Serbian President Aleksandar Vu i announced today that he met with representatives of the Bechtel-Enka company, discussing the ongoing construction of a highway that aims to connect multiple cities across Serbia. During this meeting, held on the 21st of April 2026, Vu i emphasized the importance of adhering to the project timelines, particularly for the section stretching from a ak to Kraljevo.Vu i stated, "I am particularly adamant about respecting the deadlines in the a ak-Kraljevo section, which holds significant importance for all of western and southwestern Serbia." This section is part of a larger initiative, the Moravian Corridor, that is seen as one of the most complex infrastructural projects in Serbia's recent history. The government views the timely completion of this highway as crucial for regional development and connectivity.The Bechtel-Enka consortium has faced scrutiny in the past regarding project timelines and budget overruns in various international projects. As Serbia continues to invest heavily in infrastructure development, the pressure is mounting on Bechtel-Enka to deliver as promised. Vu i 's insistence on meeting deadlines reflects the government's broader strategy to enhance economic growth through improved transportation networks.This highway project not only aims to facilitate domestic travel and commerce but also seeks to attract foreign investment by improving Serbia's infrastructure. The government believes that a well-developed transport network will contribute positively to the overall economic landscape of the country, making timely project completion all the more critical.As the Bechtel-Enka consortium moves forward with the construction, stakeholders will be closely monitoring progress to ensure that the timelines set by Vu i are met. The outcome of this project could have significant implications for both the consortium and the Serbian economy as a whole.

+ BECHTEL'S CRAIG ALBERT FUELS NEW CONSTRUCTION PROGRAM AT UNC CHARLOTTE

UNC Charlotte alumni Craig and Darla Albert have made a significant leadership gift to the university, aimed at launching a new model for construction education in one of the nation’s fastest-growing markets. This initiative will be housed within The Albert School of Construction in the William States Lee College of Engineering. The school will emphasize the full capital project lifecycle, preparing students to lead the planning, engineering, and delivery of complex infrastructure projects. This announcement comes on the 7th of April, 2026.Chancellor Sharon L. Gaber highlighted the importance of this investment, stating, "This investment allows us to align education more directly with the needs of our region and the nation." As Charlotte and North Carolina continue to grow, the complexity of infrastructure demands increases, necessitating graduates who are equipped to contribute effectively. Craig Albert, the president and chief operating officer of Bechtel Group, emphasized the pressing need for a skilled workforce to support essential industries, noting a real shortage of construction capacity.The Albert School of Construction aims to build upon the university’s 50 years of construction education by integrating disciplines across engineering, business, and data science into a cohesive academic framework. Rob Keynton, dean of the William States Lee College of Engineering, explained that the program’s unique approach encompasses the entire capital project lifecycle. This comprehensive perspective positions UNC Charlotte as a leader in the evolution of construction education, emphasizing industry partnerships and research strengths.Leading the school will be Executive Director Lingguang Song, who has a background as a professor and department chair in engineering technology and construction management. By offering a curriculum that aligns closely with industry needs, the program aspires to ensure graduates can adapt quickly to professional demands. Craig Albert stated, "My hope is that companies will see graduates from this program climbing the learning curve more quickly. They’ll be equipped for exactly what the industry needs."The Alberts’ leadership gift marks the initial phase of a broader initiative to expand the school’s facilities, programs, and industry collaborations. Future plans include the development of hands-on learning environments, an integrated curriculum with industry input, and endowed faculty positions in critical construction areas. The Alberts are also co-chairs of the For the Love of Charlotte campaign, initiated to raise a record $500 million for the university. Craig Albert expressed a desire for other successful alumni to follow their lead in supporting UNC Charlotte’s growth and impact.

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION PARTNERS IN $7.1 BILLION AUSTIN LIGHT RAIL PROJECT

A joint venture between Kiewit Building Group and Austin Commercial has secured a significant design-build contract to construct an operations and maintenance facility for the US$7.1 billion Austin Light Rail line. This announcement was made on 28th April 2026, marking a pivotal moment in the development of public transit infrastructure in Austin, Texas.The planned railway system will encompass 15 stations along a 10-mile stretch, featuring all-electric trains expected to run every five to ten minutes throughout most of the day. Kiewit Corporation, in collaboration with local partner Austin Commercial, will oversee the design and construction of the facility where Austin’s light rail vehicles will be stored, serviced, and dispatched, ensuring operational efficiency.In addition to storage and maintenance, the facility will provide a workspace for operators, maintenance crews, and support staff, thereby contributing to the overall functionality of the light rail system. The selected team for this project includes 10 subcontractors focused on the design phase, which is anticipated to generate thousands of jobs during both the design and construction phases.Greg Canally, CEO of the Austin Transit Partnership (ATP), emphasized the importance of this development, stating, "This is another major step forward, and we now have the full construction team that will deliver Austin’s light rail system. The Operations and Maintenance Facility is integral to the success of the system—it’s where trains are maintained, cleaned and prepared for service each day." Canally highlighted that partnering with a world-class construction firm like Kiewit Corporation, which possesses deep local experience, positions the project for long-term success while fostering job creation and economic opportunities across Central Texas.Previously, an Aecom-led consortium named LINC Austin was awarded the role of delivery partner for the first phase of the larger rail project in December 2024. This consortium includes notable firms such as Parsons, STV, Turner & Townsend, Ardmore Roderick, and CAS Consulting, all of which will play a crucial role in the realization of Austin's light rail ambitions.

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION SELECTED FOR EAST AUSTIN LIGHT RAIL PROJECT

A major piece of Austin's long-discussed light rail is finally moving from plans to prep work. On Wednesday, the Austin Transit Partnership signed off on Kiewit Austin Partnership as the design-build contractor for the light rail Operations and Maintenance Facility, clearing the way for pre-construction and inching a central Project Connect component closer to groundbreakers and bulldozers near the airport. The date of this significant development is 21st April 2026.According to Austin Transit Partnership, the ATP board approved Kiewit Austin Partnership—a joint venture of Kiewit Building Group Inc. and Austin Commercial—as the design-build contractor for the Operations and Maintenance Facility. ATP CEO Greg Canally remarked, “This is another major step forward, and we now have the full construction team that will deliver Austin’s light rail system.” The agency's decision allows KAP to immediately launch pre-construction work, including advancing design, starting the permitting process, and preparing the site for future construction.The operations and maintenance facility is slated for a 62-acre site at the southwest edge of the Montopolis neighborhood, according to ATP. The planned footprint, near U.S. Highway 183 and State Highway 71, will house storage and servicing for up to 40 light rail vehicles and will necessitate property acquisitions affecting dozens of existing businesses. Local reporting has highlighted that roughly one-third of the businesses expected to be displaced are concentrated in Airport Commerce Park, raising concerns about relocation and neighborhood impacts.With KAP in place, ATP plans to expedite the Operations and Maintenance Facility design through permitting and site preparation, paving the way for the broader light rail project to advance toward full construction. Industry estimates indicate that mainline construction could commence in 2027, with initial service targeted for the early 2030s. The Federal Transit Administration's combined Final Environmental Impact Statement and Record of Decision, published in January 2026, has cleared the project to enter final design and begin land acquisition.ATP noted that the KAP team already includes around 10 subcontractors during the design phase, with opportunities expected to increase once construction ramps up. The larger Project Connect program is projected to create or support thousands—potentially tens of thousands—of jobs across Texas during design and construction. On the operations side, the maintenance facility is expected to support approximately 300 long-term jobs once the system is operational, further contributing to local economic growth.

+ ATERNIUM ENGAGES KIEWIT FOR HYDROGEN PRODUCTION FACILITY PRE-FEED

Aternium has appointed Kiewit Engineering Group Inc. to undertake the pre-front-end engineering and design (pre-FEED) for its inaugural integrated hydrogen and heavy water production facility. This strategic move comes as Aternium aims to advance its ambitious $1 billion plan to produce clean hydrogen and critical heavy water domestically. The announcement was made on 26th March 2026, signaling a significant step in the company's broader, multi-plant development strategy focused on establishing a robust infrastructure for advanced energy materials.The planned facility will be located in the Mid-Atlantic region of the United States, designed to meet the growing demand for low-carbon energy solutions. Aternium's initiative not only seeks to enhance domestic capabilities in the production of heavy water—currently sourced primarily from countries like China and Iran—but also aims to address critical supply chain vulnerabilities. Andrew Cottone, Founder and CEO of Aternium, emphasized the importance of this collaboration with Kiewit, citing shared values in technical expertise and safety standards.According to Cottone, the initiative is poised to create significant socioeconomic benefits by fostering workforce development and job creation in local communities. Aternium's focus on integrating advanced technologies and innovative processes is expected to stimulate regional economic growth, thereby supporting the transition to cleaner energy systems. Kiewit will play a pivotal role in executing Aternium's unique business model, which aims to deliver both clean hydrogen and heavy water at competitive costs.Kiewit’s involvement in the project brings extensive experience in engineering, procurement, and construction within the energy sector. Tyler Nordquist, Executive Vice President at Kiewit Engineering Group Inc., expressed confidence in the partnership, highlighting the company's ability to add substantial value during the pre-FEED phase. This phase will include defining the project's scope, optimizing design parameters, and laying a solid foundation for future development.Aternium's integrated production approach is noteworthy as it combines clean hydrogen generation with a patent-pending process for isolating heavy water. This dual-output model not only aims to reduce hydrogen production costs but also addresses a critical vulnerability in the U.S. supply of strategic materials. With the increasing demand for clean energy and high-tech components, Aternium's initiative is strategically positioned at the intersection of energy transition and advanced manufacturing, thus offering a scalable solution that aligns with both economic and technological priorities.

DPR Construction, a leading regional construction firm, is set to expand its office space in Innsbrook by 9,100 square feet. The announcement comes as part of a strategic move to accommodate the company's growing workforce and enhance its operational capabilities. Local officials have expressed optimism about the project, viewing it as a significant investment in the community's future.DPR Construction has been a prominent player in the construction industry, known for its commitment to quality and sustainability. The company's decision to expand its presence in Innsbrook reflects its confidence in the local market and the potential for continued growth. The support from Henrico County is part of broader efforts to enhance the business environment, making it easier for companies to invest and thrive.As part of the expansion, DPR Construction plans to implement modern design features and sustainable practices in the new office layout. + DPR CONSTRUCTION APPOINTS JASON PAGE TO LEAD NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP

DPR Construction has announced the appointment of Jason Page as the new lead for its Special Services Group in Nashville. As of 1st April 2026, Page brings over 14 years of experience to the role, having joined DPR Construction in January 2012. His career has been marked by a versatile portfolio, encompassing projects from minor renovations to large-scale, complex developments.Since relocating to Nashville in April 2021, Jason Page has successfully spearheaded numerous corporate interior and entertainment projects, consistently delivering high-quality results under tight deadlines. His approach emphasizes a client-first leadership style, which has allowed him to cultivate strong relationships across various market sectors in the region. Page's passion for renovation and tenant improvement work has positioned him as a key player in DPR Construction's local operations.The DPR Special Services Group (SSG) plays a pivotal role in executing a diverse range of projects, from targeted improvements like parking garage stair installations to expansive interior buildouts for new office and entertainment spaces. Under Page's leadership, the SSG is expected to enhance its capabilities in meeting the evolving needs of clients and stakeholders in Nashville.In addition to project leadership, Jason Page has been actively involved in mentoring and coaching project teams within DPR Construction. He has shared best practices for executing quick-turn, high-impact work, fostering a culture of collaboration and innovation. His dedication to developing talent within the organization is seen as a valuable asset as DPR Construction continues to expand its footprint in Nashville.With Jason Page at the helm of the Special Services Group, DPR Construction is well-positioned to leverage his expertise and leadership as it navigates the competitive landscape of Nashville's construction market. The company's commitment to delivering exceptional service and quality projects remains steadfast, supported by Page's extensive experience and strategic vision.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ACHIEVES GREAT PLACE TO WORK® CERTIFICATION AMID HIGH EMPLOYEE SATISFACTION

DPR Construction, a leader in the technical building sector, has secured Great Place To Work® Certification, with an impressive 97 percent of its employees affirming that it is a great place to work. This remarkable statistic is notably 40 points higher than the typical score given to workplaces across the United States. The certification, awarded on 31st March 2026, underscores DPR's commitment to fostering a workplace culture characterized by pride, belonging, and opportunity.George Pfeffer, CEO of DPR Construction, emphasized the significance of this certification, which reflects the sentiments of the employees who embody the company's culture. He stated, "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life." Pfeffer highlighted the company's focus on creating an inclusive environment where employees feel trusted, supported, and encouraged to grow, thus enabling them to perform at their best.DPR Construction's operational philosophy is built on empowering employees across its various projects to make decisions and share information openly. This approach encourages initiative, whether it involves innovating on complex tasks, stepping into new roles, or contributing ideas beyond formal job descriptions. Employees benefit from career paths aligned with their aspirations, supported by various learning and development programs.The research conducted by Great Place To Work® corroborates DPR's employee-centric strategy, revealing that a significant portion of the workforce feels a sense of pride in their contributions and a strong sense of belonging within the company. The certification further enhances DPR's reputation as an employer of choice, with additional accolades from U.S. News & World Report and Newsweek recognizing it as one of the best companies to work for, particularly in over 15 U.S. cities over the past year.DPR Construction has built a strong legacy since its founding in 1990, evolving into a multi-billion-dollar organization that specializes in complex and sustainable projects across various sectors. + PARSONS CORPORATION EXPANDS ROLE IN NEW YORK'S INFRASTRUCTURE DEVELOPMENTS

Parsons Corporation (NYSE:PSN) has been selected to manage the construction of the Newtown Creek Combined Sewer Overflow Storage Tunnel in New York City. This announcement comes on the heels of a significant contract awarded on the 14th of April 2026, which highlights the company's commitment to innovative infrastructure solutions. As part of this project, Parsons Corporation will oversee the development of a large-diameter storage tunnel and a dewatering pump station aimed at protecting local waterways from untreated discharges.The New York City Department of Environmental Protection (DEP) has entrusted Parsons Corporation with this critical task, recognizing the firm's extensive experience in managing over 250 tunnel and ocean outfall projects worldwide. This infrastructure initiative is not only intended to modernize New York City's physical assets but also to enhance public health and improve water quality throughout the region. By leveraging its technology-driven approach, Parsons Corporation aims to deliver effective solutions that are essential for urban sustainability.In addition to its work in New York, Parsons Corporation has further solidified its position in the defense sector. A joint venture with GSI Americas has secured a $25 million contract with the United States Coast Guard (USCG). This agreement focuses on providing program management services that are vital to the USCG’s Execution Plan, which includes significant upgrades to shore infrastructure and the implementation of transformational technologies.The partnership with GSI Americas underscores Parsons Corporation's strategic intent to support federal agencies in securing and modernizing critical digital and physical assets. This multifaceted approach not only enhances the operational capabilities of the USCG but also aligns with the company's broader objectives in the defense and cybersecurity markets.Overall, Parsons Corporation's recent contracts in both infrastructure and defense sectors demonstrate its versatility and commitment to delivering integrated solutions that meet the evolving needs of urban environments and national security. As the firm continues to expand its portfolio, investors may find interest in its potential for future growth and innovation in these key areas.

+ PARSONS TEAMS WITH GSIA FOR $25 MILLION US COAST GUARD CONTRACT

CHANTILLY, Va. — Parsons Corporation (NYSE: PSN) announced today that its joint venture with GSI Americas Inc. (GSIA) has been awarded a contract valued at $25 million by the United States Coast Guard (USCG) for program management services. This contract, granted on 15th April 2026, includes a one-year base period of performance with four additional one-year option periods.The scope of this contract entails providing program management services that will facilitate the USCG’s ambitious Force Design 2028 Execution Plan, which represents the largest capital investment in the history of the Coast Guard. This comprehensive initiative aims to enhance USCG capabilities through significant upgrades to assets, technology, and shore infrastructure, supported by a total budget of $24.5 billion allocated for modernizing platforms and systems, improving training facilities, and boosting mission-critical operational capacities.Martin Boson, president of Engineered Systems for Parsons, expressed enthusiasm for the partnership, stating, "We look forward to leveraging our team’s combined global program management experience and project delivery success to support the U.S. Coast Guard in this historic modernization effort that strengthens readiness and builds a more agile, capable, and responsive force." As a leader in program management, Parsons brings a wealth of expertise and integrated services essential for the successful execution of this significant plan.GSIA, known for its innovative and sustainable energy solutions, will also play a pivotal role in this joint venture. Michael Warminsky, senior vice president for GSIA, commented, "We are proud to support the U.S. Coast Guard’s Force Design 2028 Execution Plan alongside Parsons, contributing to a program that enhances operational readiness, modernizes critical infrastructure, and strengthens the nation’s maritime security posture."With a strong track record in managing complex projects, Parsons is recognized as the top program management services firm by Engineering News-Record. The company provides comprehensive support throughout the project lifecycle, including master planning, design, construction management, and asset management. This partnership with GSIA further underscores Parsons' commitment to delivering impactful solutions in national security and infrastructure.

+ PARSONS CORPORATION WINS $409M ROOSEVELT BRIDGE DESIGN PROJECT

Parsons Corporation (NYSE:PSN) has been selected as the lead designer for the Roosevelt Bridge Replacement Project, a significant infrastructure initiative valued at $409 million. The announcement was made on 14th April 2026, detailing the project’s aim to replace an aging crossing over Lake Texoma in Bryan and Marshall Counties, Oklahoma.The Oklahoma Department of Transportation has awarded the construction contract to the Zachry Construction/Traylor Brothers Construction Joint Venture, while Parsons will handle the design aspects of the new bridge. This structure will span approximately two miles over one of the largest reservoirs in the United States, which is managed by the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers. The design will include 63 spans, with substructure elements extending about 150 feet below the lake surface.To facilitate ongoing traffic flow during construction, the project will be executed in two phases. The original Roosevelt Bridge, constructed in 1945, serves as a vital connector for local residents, freight, and travelers. The replacement initiative has secured Oklahoma’s largest federal grant amounting to $124 million in 2024, complemented by state funding.Despite the recent contract win, Parsons’ stock has experienced a decline of 35% over the past six months, currently trading at $56.01, with a market capitalization of approximately $6 billion. Analysts have indicated that the stock may be undervalued relative to its fair value, positioning it as a potential opportunity for investors. The company’s president of Infrastructure North America, Mark Fialkowski, emphasized Parsons’ legacy in bridge design, stating that this project exemplifies their commitment to delivering complex infrastructure solutions worldwide.In addition to the Roosevelt Bridge project, Parsons has been active in various significant initiatives, including a 10-year contract with the Utah Department of Transportation for a Vehicle-to-Everything Maintenance and Management System. Furthermore, Parsons is leading the design for a $389 million project on Interstate 64 in Virginia and has been appointed as the project management consultant for the Al Ittihad Sports Village in Saudi Arabia. Despite these advancements, Baird recently downgraded Parsons’ stock rating to Neutral, citing concerns over potential revenue declines in the Middle East, illustrating the complex landscape the company navigates in its growth trajectory.
