value.Title: ZACHRY GROUP - COMPREHENSIVE NEWS FROM INDUSTRY AND ITS PEERS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHN-7S43-SK5D-33CG-00000-00ZACHRY GROUP - COMPREHENSIVE NEWS FROM INDUSTRY AND ITS PEERS2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:09:17ZLexisNexisSTOCKZACHRY GROUP - COMPREHENSIVE NEWS FROM INDUSTRY AND ITS PEERS

DASHBOARD: ZACHRY GROUP

Websitehttps://www.zachrygroup.com/IndustryConstrustion

ACTIVITIES:

Zachry Group, founded in 1924, is a leading construction and engineering firm headquartered in San Antonio, Texas. Employing over 20,000 people, it provides turnkey services in power, energy, chemicals, and industrial sectors, generating over $3 billion annually. Its subsidiaries, like JVIC, offer maintenance and fabrication. In 2024, Zachry expanded renewable energy projects, including solar and wind facilities. Its culture emphasizes safety, citizenship, and employee training, with a commitment to community health and ethical practices. Competing with Fluor, Zachry stands out for its EPC (engineering, procurement, construction) expertise and safety standards. Recent initiatives include digital project management tools and carbon-neutral construction goals, aligning with sustainability trends.

+ ZACHRY GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ ZACHRY CONSTRUCTION JOINS FORCES WITH TRAYLOR BROS. FOR US-70 ROOSEVELT BRIDGE UPGRADE IN OKLAHOMA

Zachry Construction Corporation, in a joint venture with Traylor Bros., Inc., has secured a contract to replace the US-70 Roosevelt Bridge in Oklahoma, marking a strategic expansion into the state. The Oklahoma Department of Transportation selected the Zachry-Traylor JV to lead the project, the first collaboration between Zachry Construction and the OklahomaDOT.The announcement, made on 28th April 2026, highlights a major upgrade to a corridor connecting Bryan and Marshall counties and serving Lake Texoma travelers. The existing Roosevelt Bridge has operated since 1945 and remains a critical link for local communities and businesses.The plan calls for a four-lane crossing with bicycle and pedestrian accommodations, a median barrier, and upgraded lighting, with construction staged to demolish the existing bridge while keeping traffic on the bridge during the work to minimize disruption.The project team combines Zachry's long history in heavy and highway construction with Traylor Bros.' proficiency in complex bridge projects, positioning the teams to deliver a modern crossing that maintains mobility during construction and supports regional growth.Completion is targeted for 1st October 2029, reflecting a broader effort to bolster safety, reliability, and resilience in the region and to support local commerce and tourism.

+ TRAVIS MROSS ELEVATED TO PRESIDENT AT ZACHRY CONSTRUCTION, SIGNALING STRATEGIC TRANSFORMATION

Travis Mross has been named president of Zachry Construction Corp., with his official start in the role on 29th April 2026. The move marks a formal step in the leadership transition at the San Antonio-based, family-owned contractor, where Mross previously served as executive vice president for six years.Since joining the firm as an intern in 2000, Mross has played a central role in shaping the company's strategic direction and bolstering operational efficiency, according to company leaders.He succeeds David Zachry, who will continue to serve as CEO. David Zachry said, "Travis has been instrumental in setting our course and building the strong trajectory we are on today," underscoring Mross's deep understanding of the business and his ability to instill discipline.Zachry Construction is recognized for heavy civil, roadway and infrastructure work, with more than 6,000 completed initiatives across public and private sectors. The company is expanding its water and wastewater infrastructure business, a fast-growing segment, and recently acquired Crescent Constructors Inc. to broaden its capabilities in that arena.As president, Mross will oversee operations across water, transportation, underground utilities and civil sitework. His priorities are strategic growth, operational excellence and cultivating a disciplined, high-performance culture. He said, "Our job is to bring consistency, discipline, and a high standard of execution to every project, which will guide how we serve clients and grow the business."

+ ZACHRY PURCHASES 1.2-ACRE PARCEL CLUSTER TO BACK MONARCH SAN ANTONIO OPERATIONS

On Apr. 29, 2026, Zachry Group, through its affiliate Zachry Park Garage QOZB LLC, completed a ground lease with St. John's Evangelical Lutheran Church for a parking lot across South Presa Street from the church near La Villita and Hemisfair. In a separate transaction, the group acquired a smaller parking lot from St. Johns Four Square LLC, expanding the total area to about 1.2 acres. The sites were previously connected to Dennis McDaniel, the Austin developer who had planned an apartment complex there before the pandemic disrupted that plan, a larger project originally envisaged with Opportunity Home San Antonio for 252 apartments and retail space.A Zachry spokesperson said the parking assets will support operational needs for the Monarch San Antonio hotel, which opened recently overlooking Civic Park. There are no plans for further development on these parcels, underscoring a focus on hospitality operations rather than new construction.Zachry Group has deep ties to San Antonio, dating to its founding in 1924 by Henry Bartell 'Pat' Zachry, and its work on the Monarch project underscores the firm's continued role in the city’s growth. The Monarch hotel project is valued at roughly $185 million and is a flagship example of Zachry's involvement in both construction and hospitality in the region.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BECHTEL [3 News That Matters]

+ BECHTEL PARTNERS WITH SOWELA TO STRENGTHEN LOCAL PIPEFITTING SKILLS PIPELINE

Bechtel and SOWELA Technical Community College are launching a no-cost pipefitting training program at SOWELA's Main Campus in Lake Charles, designed to build a local pipeline of skilled workers for manufacturing, energy, and building systems. The program runs from 26th May 2026 through 13th November 2026.The program provides an introduction to the pipefitting trade, including core responsibilities, safety principles, and the tools and materials commonly used on the job. Trainees will gain hands-on experience with high- and low-pressure piping systems that support plants, facilities, and infrastructure.Classes meet Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 3 p.m., with completion yielding an OSHA 10-hour safety credential. Participants will be prepared to pursue NCCER Pipefitting certifications, which can boost employability in a competitive local job market.To be eligible for employment consideration, completers must pass a background check and drug screening. Interested residents can learn more or register at www.sowela.edu/pipefitting, and can reach SOWELA's Office of Workforce Solutions at (337) 421-6560 or workforce@sowela.edu for questions.The Bechtel-SOWELA collaboration underscores a shared commitment to workforce development in Lake Charles, addressing rising demand for skilled labor in construction and manufacturing and supporting regional economic growth.

+ UNC CHARLOTTE LAUNCHES ALBERT SCHOOL OF CONSTRUCTION WITH MAJOR BECHTEL GIFT

UNC Charlotte alumni Craig and Darla Albert have given a leadership gift to establish the Albert School of Construction within the William States Lee College of Engineering, launching a new model for construction education in one of the nation ’s fastest-growing markets. The announcement was made Apr. 7, 2026. The school will focus on the full capital project lifecycle, training students to lead from planning and engineering through delivery of complex infrastructure. Chancellor Sharon L. Gaber noted that the investment helps align education with regional and national needs. Bechtel Group’s Craig Albert underscored the industry’s demand for skilled workers, citing a real shortage of construction capacity.

+ LUCY MARTIN TO LEAD BECHTEL’S MINING & METALS AS AILIE MACADAM RETIRES

Bechtel named Lucy Martin as president of its Mining & Metals (M&M) business, effective April 6, 2026, as Ailie MacAdam prepares to retire in May after a 41-year Bechtel career. Craig Albert, Bechtel President and COO, stated on 3rd April 2026, "Lucy is a proven leader with the strategic vision, deep mining sector knowledge, operational discipline, and customer focus to advance our Mining & Metals business." Martin has spent over two decades at Bechtel, where she has developed high-performing global teams aimed at solving complex challenges for customers across various markets. In her new role, Martin said she intends to build on Bechtel's momentum in mining, acknowledging a rapidly evolving global market. "Our focus will be on enhancing how we deliver results for our customers, particularly in areas such as cost-effective development, productivity, safety, reliability, and certainty of outcomes," she added. MacAdam's tenure delivered major project progress in key markets and led to the highest new-work bookings in 15 years for Mining & Metals in 2025. Bechtel said it remains a trusted partner across sectors including Energy, Infrastructure, Manufacturing & Technology, Mining & Metals, and Nuclear, and will continue to address the demands of a changing energy landscape under its new leader.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ KIEWIT-AUSTIN COMMERCIAL JV TO DELIVER OPERATIONS & MAINTENANCE FACILITY FOR AUSTIN LIGHT RAIL

On 28th April 2026, a joint venture between Kiewit Building Group and Austin Commercial secured a design-build contract to construct an operations and maintenance facility for the US$7.1 billion Austin Light Rail line, which will span 15 stations along a 10-mile corridor and feature all-electric trains expected to run every five to ten minutes through much of the day.Kiewit and Austin Commercial will design and build the facility where Austin’s light rail vehicles will be stored, serviced, and dispatched to ensure reliable daily service.In addition to storage and maintenance, the facility will provide workspace for operators, maintenance crews, and support staff, contributing to the overall functionality of the system.The project includes about 10 design-stage subcontractors and is expected to generate thousands of jobs during both the design and construction phases.ATP Chief Executive Greg Canally said, "This is another major step forward, and we now have the full construction team that will deliver Austin’s light rail system. The Operations and Maintenance Facility is integral to the success of the system—it’s where trains are maintained, cleaned and prepared for service each day." Canally added that partnering with Kiewit, a world-class firm with deep local experience, positions the project for long-term success and broader economic opportunities across Central Texas. The LINC Austin program, initially led by Aecom, includes Parsons, STV, Turner & Townsend, Ardmore Roderick, and CAS Consulting among its delivery-partner collaborators.

+ AUSTIN TRANSIT PARTNERSHIP NAMES KAP TO LEAD OMF FOR PROJECT CONNECT

The Austin Transit Partnership's board has chosen Kiewit Austin Partnership, a joint venture of Kiewit Building Group Inc. and Austin Commercial, as the design-build contractor for the Operations and Maintenance Facility, enabling immediate pre-construction activities such as design advancement, permitting, and site preparation. The decision was announced on 21st April 2026.The OMF footprint covers about 62 acres on the southwest edge of the Montopolis neighborhood, near U.S. 183 and SH 71, and will house storage and servicing for up to 40 light rail vehicles. To make room for the facility, property acquisitions will affect dozens of existing businesses, with relocation concerns centered on Airport Commerce Park.KAP currently brings roughly 10 subcontractors into the design phase, with more work expected as construction ramps up. The broader Project Connect program is projected to create or support thousands—potentially tens of thousands—of jobs across Texas during design and construction. The OMF is expected to support about 300 long-term jobs once the system is operational, contributing to local economic growth. Mainline construction is expected to start in 2027, with initial service targeted for the early 2030s. The Federal Transit Administration published its final Environmental Impact Statement and Record of Decision in January 2026, allowing the project to move into final design and land acquisition.

+ KIEWIT TO LEAD PRE-FEED FOR ATERNIUM'S $1 BILLION HYDROGEN AND HEAVY WATER PLANT

Aternium has appointed Kiewit Engineering Group Inc. to undertake the pre-front-end engineering and design (pre-FEED) for its first integrated hydrogen and heavy water production facility, a step in its roughly $1 billion plan to produce clean hydrogen and critical heavy water domestically. The announcement was made on 26th March 2026.The Mid-Atlantic site is designed to meet rising demand for low-carbon energy and strengthen domestic capabilities for heavy water, which is currently sourced primarily from countries such as China and Iran. Andrew Cottone, founder and CEO of Aternium, noted that the collaboration with Kiewit aligns with the company's emphasis on technical excellence and safety standards.During the pre-FEED phase, Kiewit will help define the project scope, optimize design parameters, and lay a solid foundation for future development, according to the companies. Tyler Nordquist, executive vice president at Kiewit Engineering Group Inc., said the partnership will add substantial value in this early stage.Aternium's integrated production approach combines clean hydrogen generation with a patent-pending process for isolating heavy water, a dual output designed to lower hydrogen production costs and to address vulnerabilities in the U.S. supply of strategic materials. The project sits at the intersection of energy transition and advanced manufacturing and aims to deliver scalable benefits to local communities through workforce development and job creation.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION TO GROW INNSBROOK FOOTPRINT WITH 9,100-SF EXPANSION BACKED BY HENRICO COUNTY

DPR Construction announced a 9,100-square-foot expansion of its Innsbrook office, supported by Henrico County incentives to attract and retain businesses. The date of this announcement is 29th April 2026.The expansion aims to accommodate a growing workforce and strengthen operational capabilities as part of the company’s regional growth strategy.The project will bring together teams and resources, promoting collaboration and innovation across the organization.Sustainable design features and modern office concepts will be integrated, aligning with DPR’s leadership in sustainable construction.Completion is expected within the next year, underscoring the company’s commitment to the region and its ongoing community initiatives.

+ JASON PAGE APPOINTED HEAD OF DPR CONSTRUCTION'S NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP

The Special Services Group handles a broad range of work, from targeted improvements like parking garage stair installations to expansive interior buildouts for new office and entertainment spaces, and Page's leadership is expected to expand the group's capabilities to meet evolving client and stakeholder needs in Nashville. Beyond project leadership, Page mentors and coaches teams, sharing best practices for quick-turn, high-impact work and fostering a culture of collaboration and innovation as DPR expands its Nashville footprint.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION SECURES GREAT PLACE TO WORK STATUS AS STAFF REPORT HIGH ENGAGEMENT

DPR Construction has secured Great Place To Work Certification, confirming that 97 percent of its employees say it is a great place to work, a figure about 40 points higher than the national average. The award, presented on 31st March 2026, signals a culture rooted in pride, belonging and opportunity across the firm’s projects.CEO George Pfeffer noted the certification reflects sentiments across the organization. "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life," he said. The company emphasizes trust, support, and growth to help employees perform at their best.DPR’s operating model empowers staff to make decisions and share information openly, encouraging initiative and cross functional collaboration on complex tasks, new roles, and ideas beyond job descriptions. A strong emphasis on learning and development supports clear career paths aligned with individual aspirations.Great Place To Work® research aligns with these findings, highlighting a sense of pride and belonging among workers. The certification has helped DPR strengthen its reputation as an employer of choice, while external accolades from U.S. News & World Report and Newsweek have recognized DPR in more than 15 U.S. cities over the last year.Founded in 1990, DPR has evolved into a multi billion dollar contractor known for complex and sustainable projects across sectors. Its innovation posture—through virtual design and construction and self performing craft—helps explain its roughly 11,000 person workforce worldwide. More information about DPR’s culture and career opportunities is available on its official website.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PARSONS CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ PARSONS CORPORATION (NYSE:PSN) JOINS WITH EVERYWHERE COMMUNICATIONS TO DEVELOP RESILIENT AUTONOMOUS DRONE PLATFORM

Parsons Corporation (NYSE:PSN) and EVERYWHERE Communications forged a strategic partnership to accelerate autonomous drone development under the SBIR initiative, targeting operations in degraded and disconnected environments. The partnership was confirmed on 4th of May 2026. The collaboration leverages Iridium Satellite technology to deliver a resilient data transport layer that lets drones operate autonomously while streaming essential sensor data back to operators and command systems. The system supports beyond-line-of-sight operations, reliable data exfiltration and autonomous mission execution, with scalable coordination for fleets of drones on low-bandwidth links. Jake Bailey, President of EVERYWHERE Communications, notes, "In austere environments, connectivity is never guaranteed. We're enabling drones to keep operating—and keep delivering intelligence—even when the network is compromised." Parsons contributes TAK-as-a-Service (TaaS) to provide scalable, mission-ready integration and sustainment services tailored for the Department of War and federal agencies, enhancing real-time situational awareness and data interoperability. Mike Kushin, President of Defense and Intelligence at Parsons, adds, "In an era where every second and every signal matters, this collaboration brings together resilient autonomy in the air and trusted mission systems on the ground to give our warfighters and intelligence professionals a decisive information advantage." The alliance seeks to redefine operational capabilities for mission-critical teams worldwide.

+ PARSONS CORPORATION (NYSE: PSN) SETS DELEGATED PROGRAM MANAGER ROLE FOR THE BORING COMPANY'S DUBAI LOOP

CHANTILLY, Va. — The Boring Company awards Parsons a nine-month contract for the Dubai Loop project, dated 5th May 2026Parsons will serve as TBC’s delegated program manager, overseeing design-build activities for the Dubai Loop project and delivering Independent Design Verification, Stakeholder Management, and Permitting and NOC Support for the pilot phase. The firm will also perform multidisciplinary reviews of designs across civil, structural, mechanical, electrical, safety, and utility disciplinesPierre Santoni, president of Infrastructure EMEA at Parsons, highlighted the UAE's ongoing infrastructure portfolio and stated, "Parsons has proudly played a central role in developing some of the most important and prestigious critical infrastructure programs across the UAE since our first programs there in the 1980s." He added that the partnership with TBC aligns with the UAE leadership's vision to foster strategic partnerships and enhance advanced technologies as part of the Dubai 2040 Urban Master PlanThe Dubai Loop project, developed in collaboration with Dubai’s Roads and Transit Authority (RTA), aims to expand the city’s transport ecosystem through a series of passenger transport tunnels and stations. Parsons has a history of working with the RTA, having contributed to significant transportation infrastructure projects in the Emirate since 2005, including the Dubai Metro and various highway and bridge projectsParsons Corporation has managed over 250 complex tunneling projects globally and has extensive experience in road and highway development, covering more than 10,000 miles across six continents. As a provider of disruptive technologies in national security and global infrastructure, Parsons is well-positioned to contribute to the Dubai Loop initiative, enhancing urban mobility and transportation efficiency in one of the world's most dynamic cities

+ PARSONS CORPORATION (NYSE:PSN) WINS NYC DEP PROJECT FOR LARGE-DIAMETER STORAGE TUNNEL AND DEWATERING PUMP STATION AND SECURES USCG PROGRAM MANAGEMENT CONTRACT VIA GSI AMERICAS JV

Parsons Corporation (NYSE:PSN) Posts Progress On Apr. 14, 2026 Contract Award To Lead The Construction Of The Newtown Creek CSO Storage Tunnel In New York City. The Project Features A Large-Diameter Storage Tunnel And A Dewatering Pump Station To Curb Untreated DischargesThe New York City Department Of Environmental Protection Has Entrusted Parsons With This Critical Task, Citing The Firm's Experience On More Than 250 Tunnel And Ocean Outfall Projects Worldwide. The Initiative Aims To Modernize Municipal Assets, Improve Water Quality, And Support Urban Sustainability Through A Technology-Driven ApproachIn Addition, Parsons Forms A Joint Venture With GSI Americas That Secures A US Coast Guard Program Management Contract Worth $25 Million. The Contract Supports The USCG Execution Plan, Including Significant Upgrades To Shore Infrastructure And Deployment Of Transformational TechnologiesThe Partnership Highlights Parsons' Strategic Intent To Assist Federal Agencies In Securing And Modernizing Critical Digital And Physical Assets. The Arrangement Aligns With The Company's Broader Focus On Defense And Cybersecurity Markets And Reflects Its Ability To Deliver Integrated Solutions For Urban And National Security ChallengesInvestors May Monitor The Company's Ongoing Growth Across Infrastructure And Defense Initiatives As Parsons Expands Its Portfolio

urn:contentItem:6JHF-8HY3-S51B-P1BJ-00000-00ZACHRY GROUP - THE RECENT ON INDUSTRY AND PEER DEVELOPMENTS2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:09:17ZLexisNexisSTOCKZACHRY GROUP - THE RECENT ON INDUSTRY AND PEER DEVELOPMENTS

DASHBOARD: ZACHRY GROUP

Websitehttps://www.zachrygroup.com/IndustryConstrustion

ACTIVITIES:

Zachry Group, founded in 1924, is a leading construction and engineering firm headquartered in San Antonio, Texas. Employing over 20,000 people, it provides turnkey services in power, energy, chemicals, and industrial sectors, generating over $3 billion annually. Its subsidiaries, like JVIC, offer maintenance and fabrication. In 2024, Zachry expanded renewable energy projects, including solar and wind facilities. Its culture emphasizes safety, citizenship, and employee training, with a commitment to community health and ethical practices. Competing with Fluor, Zachry stands out for its EPC (engineering, procurement, construction) expertise and safety standards. Recent initiatives include digital project management tools and carbon-neutral construction goals, aligning with sustainability trends.

+ ZACHRY GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ ZACHRY CONSTRUCTION JOINS FORCES WITH TRAYLOR BROS. FOR US-70 ROOSEVELT BRIDGE UPGRADE IN OKLAHOMA

Zachry Construction Corporation, in a joint venture with Traylor Bros., Inc., has secured a contract to replace the US-70 Roosevelt Bridge in Oklahoma, marking a strategic expansion into the state. The Oklahoma Department of Transportation selected the Zachry-Traylor JV to lead the project, the first collaboration between Zachry Construction and the OklahomaDOT.The announcement, made on 28th April 2026, highlights a major upgrade to a corridor connecting Bryan and Marshall counties and serving Lake Texoma travelers. The existing Roosevelt Bridge has operated since 1945 and remains a critical link for local communities and businesses.The plan calls for a four-lane crossing with bicycle and pedestrian accommodations, a median barrier, and upgraded lighting, with construction staged to demolish the existing bridge while keeping traffic on the bridge during the work to minimize disruption.The project team combines Zachry's long history in heavy and highway construction with Traylor Bros.' proficiency in complex bridge projects, positioning the teams to deliver a modern crossing that maintains mobility during construction and supports regional growth.Completion is targeted for 1st October 2029, reflecting a broader effort to bolster safety, reliability, and resilience in the region and to support local commerce and tourism.

+ TRAVIS MROSS ELEVATED TO PRESIDENT AT ZACHRY CONSTRUCTION, SIGNALING STRATEGIC TRANSFORMATION

Travis Mross has been named president of Zachry Construction Corp., with his official start in the role on 29th April 2026. The move marks a formal step in the leadership transition at the San Antonio-based, family-owned contractor, where Mross previously served as executive vice president for six years.Since joining the firm as an intern in 2000, Mross has played a central role in shaping the company's strategic direction and bolstering operational efficiency, according to company leaders.He succeeds David Zachry, who will continue to serve as CEO. David Zachry said, "Travis has been instrumental in setting our course and building the strong trajectory we are on today," underscoring Mross's deep understanding of the business and his ability to instill discipline.Zachry Construction is recognized for heavy civil, roadway and infrastructure work, with more than 6,000 completed initiatives across public and private sectors. The company is expanding its water and wastewater infrastructure business, a fast-growing segment, and recently acquired Crescent Constructors Inc. to broaden its capabilities in that arena.As president, Mross will oversee operations across water, transportation, underground utilities and civil sitework. His priorities are strategic growth, operational excellence and cultivating a disciplined, high-performance culture. He said, "Our job is to bring consistency, discipline, and a high standard of execution to every project, which will guide how we serve clients and grow the business."

+ ZACHRY PURCHASES 1.2-ACRE PARCEL CLUSTER TO BACK MONARCH SAN ANTONIO OPERATIONS

On Apr. 29, 2026, Zachry Group, through its affiliate Zachry Park Garage QOZB LLC, completed a ground lease with St. John's Evangelical Lutheran Church for a parking lot across South Presa Street from the church near La Villita and Hemisfair. In a separate transaction, the group acquired a smaller parking lot from St. Johns Four Square LLC, expanding the total area to about 1.2 acres. The sites were previously connected to Dennis McDaniel, the Austin developer who had planned an apartment complex there before the pandemic disrupted that plan, a larger project originally envisaged with Opportunity Home San Antonio for 252 apartments and retail space.A Zachry spokesperson said the parking assets will support operational needs for the Monarch San Antonio hotel, which opened recently overlooking Civic Park. There are no plans for further development on these parcels, underscoring a focus on hospitality operations rather than new construction.Zachry Group has deep ties to San Antonio, dating to its founding in 1924 by Henry Bartell 'Pat' Zachry, and its work on the Monarch project underscores the firm's continued role in the city’s growth. The Monarch hotel project is valued at roughly $185 million and is a flagship example of Zachry's involvement in both construction and hospitality in the region.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BECHTEL [3 News That Matters]

+ BECHTEL PARTNERS WITH SOWELA TO STRENGTHEN LOCAL PIPEFITTING SKILLS PIPELINE

Bechtel and SOWELA Technical Community College are launching a no-cost pipefitting training program at SOWELA's Main Campus in Lake Charles, designed to build a local pipeline of skilled workers for manufacturing, energy, and building systems. The program runs from 26th May 2026 through 13th November 2026.The program provides an introduction to the pipefitting trade, including core responsibilities, safety principles, and the tools and materials commonly used on the job. Trainees will gain hands-on experience with high- and low-pressure piping systems that support plants, facilities, and infrastructure.Classes meet Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 3 p.m., with completion yielding an OSHA 10-hour safety credential. Participants will be prepared to pursue NCCER Pipefitting certifications, which can boost employability in a competitive local job market.To be eligible for employment consideration, completers must pass a background check and drug screening. Interested residents can learn more or register at www.sowela.edu/pipefitting, and can reach SOWELA's Office of Workforce Solutions at (337) 421-6560 or workforce@sowela.edu for questions.The Bechtel-SOWELA collaboration underscores a shared commitment to workforce development in Lake Charles, addressing rising demand for skilled labor in construction and manufacturing and supporting regional economic growth.

+ UNC CHARLOTTE LAUNCHES ALBERT SCHOOL OF CONSTRUCTION WITH MAJOR BECHTEL GIFT

UNC Charlotte alumni Craig and Darla Albert have given a leadership gift to establish the Albert School of Construction within the William States Lee College of Engineering, launching a new model for construction education in one of the nation ’s fastest-growing markets. The announcement was made Apr. 7, 2026. The school will focus on the full capital project lifecycle, training students to lead from planning and engineering through delivery of complex infrastructure. Chancellor Sharon L. Gaber noted that the investment helps align education with regional and national needs. Bechtel Group’s Craig Albert underscored the industry’s demand for skilled workers, citing a real shortage of construction capacity.

+ LUCY MARTIN TO LEAD BECHTEL’S MINING & METALS AS AILIE MACADAM RETIRES

Bechtel named Lucy Martin as president of its Mining & Metals (M&M) business, effective April 6, 2026, as Ailie MacAdam prepares to retire in May after a 41-year Bechtel career. Craig Albert, Bechtel President and COO, stated on 3rd April 2026, "Lucy is a proven leader with the strategic vision, deep mining sector knowledge, operational discipline, and customer focus to advance our Mining & Metals business." Martin has spent over two decades at Bechtel, where she has developed high-performing global teams aimed at solving complex challenges for customers across various markets. In her new role, Martin said she intends to build on Bechtel's momentum in mining, acknowledging a rapidly evolving global market. "Our focus will be on enhancing how we deliver results for our customers, particularly in areas such as cost-effective development, productivity, safety, reliability, and certainty of outcomes," she added. MacAdam's tenure delivered major project progress in key markets and led to the highest new-work bookings in 15 years for Mining & Metals in 2025. Bechtel said it remains a trusted partner across sectors including Energy, Infrastructure, Manufacturing & Technology, Mining & Metals, and Nuclear, and will continue to address the demands of a changing energy landscape under its new leader.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ KIEWIT-AUSTIN COMMERCIAL JV TO DELIVER OPERATIONS & MAINTENANCE FACILITY FOR AUSTIN LIGHT RAIL

On 28th April 2026, a joint venture between Kiewit Building Group and Austin Commercial secured a design-build contract to construct an operations and maintenance facility for the US$7.1 billion Austin Light Rail line, which will span 15 stations along a 10-mile corridor and feature all-electric trains expected to run every five to ten minutes through much of the day.Kiewit and Austin Commercial will design and build the facility where Austin’s light rail vehicles will be stored, serviced, and dispatched to ensure reliable daily service.In addition to storage and maintenance, the facility will provide workspace for operators, maintenance crews, and support staff, contributing to the overall functionality of the system.The project includes about 10 design-stage subcontractors and is expected to generate thousands of jobs during both the design and construction phases.ATP Chief Executive Greg Canally said, "This is another major step forward, and we now have the full construction team that will deliver Austin’s light rail system. The Operations and Maintenance Facility is integral to the success of the system—it’s where trains are maintained, cleaned and prepared for service each day." Canally added that partnering with Kiewit, a world-class firm with deep local experience, positions the project for long-term success and broader economic opportunities across Central Texas. The LINC Austin program, initially led by Aecom, includes Parsons, STV, Turner & Townsend, Ardmore Roderick, and CAS Consulting among its delivery-partner collaborators.

+ AUSTIN TRANSIT PARTNERSHIP NAMES KAP TO LEAD OMF FOR PROJECT CONNECT

The Austin Transit Partnership's board has chosen Kiewit Austin Partnership, a joint venture of Kiewit Building Group Inc. and Austin Commercial, as the design-build contractor for the Operations and Maintenance Facility, enabling immediate pre-construction activities such as design advancement, permitting, and site preparation. The decision was announced on 21st April 2026.The OMF footprint covers about 62 acres on the southwest edge of the Montopolis neighborhood, near U.S. 183 and SH 71, and will house storage and servicing for up to 40 light rail vehicles. To make room for the facility, property acquisitions will affect dozens of existing businesses, with relocation concerns centered on Airport Commerce Park.KAP currently brings roughly 10 subcontractors into the design phase, with more work expected as construction ramps up. The broader Project Connect program is projected to create or support thousands—potentially tens of thousands—of jobs across Texas during design and construction. The OMF is expected to support about 300 long-term jobs once the system is operational, contributing to local economic growth. Mainline construction is expected to start in 2027, with initial service targeted for the early 2030s. The Federal Transit Administration published its final Environmental Impact Statement and Record of Decision in January 2026, allowing the project to move into final design and land acquisition.

+ KIEWIT TO LEAD PRE-FEED FOR ATERNIUM'S $1 BILLION HYDROGEN AND HEAVY WATER PLANT

Aternium has appointed Kiewit Engineering Group Inc. to undertake the pre-front-end engineering and design (pre-FEED) for its first integrated hydrogen and heavy water production facility, a step in its roughly $1 billion plan to produce clean hydrogen and critical heavy water domestically. The announcement was made on 26th March 2026.The Mid-Atlantic site is designed to meet rising demand for low-carbon energy and strengthen domestic capabilities for heavy water, which is currently sourced primarily from countries such as China and Iran. Andrew Cottone, founder and CEO of Aternium, noted that the collaboration with Kiewit aligns with the company's emphasis on technical excellence and safety standards.During the pre-FEED phase, Kiewit will help define the project scope, optimize design parameters, and lay a solid foundation for future development, according to the companies. Tyler Nordquist, executive vice president at Kiewit Engineering Group Inc., said the partnership will add substantial value in this early stage.Aternium's integrated production approach combines clean hydrogen generation with a patent-pending process for isolating heavy water, a dual output designed to lower hydrogen production costs and to address vulnerabilities in the U.S. supply of strategic materials. The project sits at the intersection of energy transition and advanced manufacturing and aims to deliver scalable benefits to local communities through workforce development and job creation.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION TO GROW INNSBROOK FOOTPRINT WITH 9,100-SF EXPANSION BACKED BY HENRICO COUNTY

DPR Construction announced a 9,100-square-foot expansion of its Innsbrook office, supported by Henrico County incentives to attract and retain businesses. The date of this announcement is 29th April 2026.The expansion aims to accommodate a growing workforce and strengthen operational capabilities as part of the company’s regional growth strategy.The project will bring together teams and resources, promoting collaboration and innovation across the organization.Sustainable design features and modern office concepts will be integrated, aligning with DPR’s leadership in sustainable construction.Completion is expected within the next year, underscoring the company’s commitment to the region and its ongoing community initiatives.

+ JASON PAGE APPOINTED HEAD OF DPR CONSTRUCTION'S NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP

The Special Services Group handles a broad range of work, from targeted improvements like parking garage stair installations to expansive interior buildouts for new office and entertainment spaces, and Page's leadership is expected to expand the group's capabilities to meet evolving client and stakeholder needs in Nashville. Beyond project leadership, Page mentors and coaches teams, sharing best practices for quick-turn, high-impact work and fostering a culture of collaboration and innovation as DPR expands its Nashville footprint.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION SECURES GREAT PLACE TO WORK STATUS AS STAFF REPORT HIGH ENGAGEMENT

DPR Construction has secured Great Place To Work Certification, confirming that 97 percent of its employees say it is a great place to work, a figure about 40 points higher than the national average. The award, presented on 31st March 2026, signals a culture rooted in pride, belonging and opportunity across the firm’s projects.CEO George Pfeffer noted the certification reflects sentiments across the organization. "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life," he said. The company emphasizes trust, support, and growth to help employees perform at their best.DPR’s operating model empowers staff to make decisions and share information openly, encouraging initiative and cross functional collaboration on complex tasks, new roles, and ideas beyond job descriptions. A strong emphasis on learning and development supports clear career paths aligned with individual aspirations.Great Place To Work® research aligns with these findings, highlighting a sense of pride and belonging among workers. The certification has helped DPR strengthen its reputation as an employer of choice, while external accolades from U.S. News & World Report and Newsweek have recognized DPR in more than 15 U.S. cities over the last year.Founded in 1990, DPR has evolved into a multi billion dollar contractor known for complex and sustainable projects across sectors. Its innovation posture—through virtual design and construction and self performing craft—helps explain its roughly 11,000 person workforce worldwide. More information about DPR’s culture and career opportunities is available on its official website.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PARSONS CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ PARSONS CORPORATION (NYSE:PSN) WINS NYC DEP PROJECT FOR LARGE-DIAMETER STORAGE TUNNEL AND DEWATERING PUMP STATION AND SECURES USCG PROGRAM MANAGEMENT CONTRACT VIA GSI AMERICAS JV

Parsons Corporation (NYSE:PSN) Posts Progress On Apr. 14, 2026 Contract Award To Lead The Construction Of The Newtown Creek CSO Storage Tunnel In New York City. The Project Features A Large-Diameter Storage Tunnel And A Dewatering Pump Station To Curb Untreated DischargesThe New York City Department Of Environmental Protection Has Entrusted Parsons With This Critical Task, Citing The Firm's Experience On More Than 250 Tunnel And Ocean Outfall Projects Worldwide. The Initiative Aims To Modernize Municipal Assets, Improve Water Quality, And Support Urban Sustainability Through A Technology-Driven ApproachIn Addition, Parsons Forms A Joint Venture With GSI Americas That Secures A US Coast Guard Program Management Contract Worth $25 Million. The Contract Supports The USCG Execution Plan, Including Significant Upgrades To Shore Infrastructure And Deployment Of Transformational TechnologiesThe Partnership Highlights Parsons' Strategic Intent To Assist Federal Agencies In Securing And Modernizing Critical Digital And Physical Assets. The Arrangement Aligns With The Company's Broader Focus On Defense And Cybersecurity Markets And Reflects Its Ability To Deliver Integrated Solutions For Urban And National Security ChallengesInvestors May Monitor The Company's Ongoing Growth Across Infrastructure And Defense Initiatives As Parsons Expands Its Portfolio

+ PARSONS CORPORATION (NYSE: PSN) REPORTS $25 MILLION US COAST GUARD PROGRAM MANAGEMENT AWARD WITH GSIA

Parsons Corporation (NYSE: PSN) and its joint venture with GSI Americas Inc. (GSIA) report a $25 million contract from the United States Coast Guard (USCG) for program management services, granted on 15th April 2026, with a one-year base period and four additional one-year option periodsThe scope of the contract will support the USCG’s Force Design 2028 Execution Plan, a comprehensive modernization effort that includes upgrades to assets, technology, and shore infrastructure. The program carries a total budget of $24.5 billion allocated for modernizing platforms and systems, improving training facilities, and strengthening mission-critical operational capacitiesMartin Boson, president of Engineered Systems for Parsons, stated, "We look forward to leveraging our team’s combined global program management experience and project delivery success to support the U.S. Coast Guard in this modernization effort that strengthens readiness and builds a more agile, capable, and responsive force." GSIA, for its part, will play a pivotal role in the joint venture. Michael Warminsky, senior vice president for GSIA, added, "We are proud to support the U.S. Coast Guard’s Force Design 2028 Execution Plan alongside Parsons, contributing to a program that enhances operational readiness, modernizes critical infrastructure, and strengthens the nation’s maritime security posture." Engineering News-Record has recognized Parsons as a provider of program management services, reinforcing the value of this collaborationThe partnership highlights Parsons’ capabilities in managing complex projects across master planning, design, construction management, and asset management, supporting national security and infrastructure initiatives

+ PARSONS CORPORATION (NYSE:PSN) NAMED LEAD DESIGNER ON ROOSEVELT BRIDGE REPLACEMENT IN $409 MILLION CONTRACT

Parsons Corporation (NYSE:PSN) has been named the lead designer for the Roosevelt Bridge Replacement Project, a $409 million contract announced on 14th April 2026. The Oklahoma Department of Transportation awarded the construction phase to the Zachry Construction/Traylor Brothers Construction Joint Venture, with Parsons responsible for the bridge design over Lake Texoma in Bryan and Marshall Counties. The project will span approximately two miles across one of the nation’s largest reservoirs, with 63 spans and substructure elements extending about 150 feet below the lake surface, under the Army Corps of Engineers oversight

urn:contentItem:6JJX-X4N3-S254-Y4NH-00000-00ZACHRY GROUP - THE RECENT NEWS OF BECHTEL CORPORATION AND PEERS2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:09:17ZLexisNexisSTOCKZACHRY GROUP - THE RECENT NEWS OF BECHTEL CORPORATION AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: ZACHRY GROUP

Websitehttps://www.zachrygroup.com/IndustryConstrustion

ACTIVITIES:

Zachry Group, founded in 1924, is a leading construction and engineering firm headquartered in San Antonio, Texas. Employing over 20,000 people, it provides turnkey services in power, energy, chemicals, and industrial sectors, generating over $3 billion annually. Its subsidiaries, like JVIC, offer maintenance and fabrication. In 2024, Zachry expanded renewable energy projects, including solar and wind facilities. Its culture emphasizes safety, citizenship, and employee training, with a commitment to community health and ethical practices. Competing with Fluor, Zachry stands out for its EPC (engineering, procurement, construction) expertise and safety standards. Recent initiatives include digital project management tools and carbon-neutral construction goals, aligning with sustainability trends.

+ ZACHRY GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ ZACHRY CONSTRUCTION JOINS FORCES WITH TRAYLOR BROS. FOR US-70 ROOSEVELT BRIDGE UPGRADE IN OKLAHOMA

Zachry Construction Corporation, in a joint venture with Traylor Bros., Inc., has secured a contract to replace the US-70 Roosevelt Bridge in Oklahoma, marking a strategic expansion into the state. The Oklahoma Department of Transportation selected the Zachry-Traylor JV to lead the project, the first collaboration between Zachry Construction and the OklahomaDOT.The announcement, made on 28th April 2026, highlights a major upgrade to a corridor connecting Bryan and Marshall counties and serving Lake Texoma travelers. The existing Roosevelt Bridge has operated since 1945 and remains a critical link for local communities and businesses.The plan calls for a four-lane crossing with bicycle and pedestrian accommodations, a median barrier, and upgraded lighting, with construction staged to demolish the existing bridge while keeping traffic on the bridge during the work to minimize disruption.The project team combines Zachry's long history in heavy and highway construction with Traylor Bros.' proficiency in complex bridge projects, positioning the teams to deliver a modern crossing that maintains mobility during construction and supports regional growth.Completion is targeted for 1st October 2029, reflecting a broader effort to bolster safety, reliability, and resilience in the region and to support local commerce and tourism.

+ TRAVIS MROSS ELEVATED TO PRESIDENT AT ZACHRY CONSTRUCTION, SIGNALING STRATEGIC TRANSFORMATION

Travis Mross has been named president of Zachry Construction Corp., with his official start in the role on 29th April 2026. The move marks a formal step in the leadership transition at the San Antonio-based, family-owned contractor, where Mross previously served as executive vice president for six years.Since joining the firm as an intern in 2000, Mross has played a central role in shaping the company's strategic direction and bolstering operational efficiency, according to company leaders.He succeeds David Zachry, who will continue to serve as CEO. David Zachry said, "Travis has been instrumental in setting our course and building the strong trajectory we are on today," underscoring Mross's deep understanding of the business and his ability to instill discipline.Zachry Construction is recognized for heavy civil, roadway and infrastructure work, with more than 6,000 completed initiatives across public and private sectors. The company is expanding its water and wastewater infrastructure business, a fast-growing segment, and recently acquired Crescent Constructors Inc. to broaden its capabilities in that arena.As president, Mross will oversee operations across water, transportation, underground utilities and civil sitework. His priorities are strategic growth, operational excellence and cultivating a disciplined, high-performance culture. He said, "Our job is to bring consistency, discipline, and a high standard of execution to every project, which will guide how we serve clients and grow the business."

+ ZACHRY PURCHASES 1.2-ACRE PARCEL CLUSTER TO BACK MONARCH SAN ANTONIO OPERATIONS

On Apr. 29, 2026, Zachry Group, through its affiliate Zachry Park Garage QOZB LLC, completed a ground lease with St. John's Evangelical Lutheran Church for a parking lot across South Presa Street from the church near La Villita and Hemisfair. In a separate transaction, the group acquired a smaller parking lot from St. Johns Four Square LLC, expanding the total area to about 1.2 acres. The sites were previously connected to Dennis McDaniel, the Austin developer who had planned an apartment complex there before the pandemic disrupted that plan, a larger project originally envisaged with Opportunity Home San Antonio for 252 apartments and retail space.A Zachry spokesperson said the parking assets will support operational needs for the Monarch San Antonio hotel, which opened recently overlooking Civic Park. There are no plans for further development on these parcels, underscoring a focus on hospitality operations rather than new construction.Zachry Group has deep ties to San Antonio, dating to its founding in 1924 by Henry Bartell 'Pat' Zachry, and its work on the Monarch project underscores the firm's continued role in the city’s growth. The Monarch hotel project is valued at roughly $185 million and is a flagship example of Zachry's involvement in both construction and hospitality in the region.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BECHTEL [3 News That Matters]

+ BECHTEL AND NABTU SIGN MOU TO EXPAND APPRENTICESHIPS FOR NUCLEAR PROJECTS

Bechtel and NABTU announced a strategic partnership to modernize apprenticeship programs for nuclear construction, signing a Memorandum of Understanding on May 5, 2026. The effort is designed to support an expanded nuclear build program in the United States, including large reactors and Small Modular Reactors (SMRs). The two organizations will work together to map the specialized craft skills needed for nuclear projects and ensure that apprenticeship training aligns with the technical demands and quality standards of modern plants. Curriculum modernization will take place at union training centers across North America, updating courses to reflect cutting-edge methods and technologies used in today’s reactors and related facilities. Recruitment will be coordinated through NABTU's union networks to widen pathways into high-skill careers and help fill roles in building large reactors and SMRs. Bechtel Chair and CEO Brendan Bechtel highlighted the collaboration’s potential to maintain safety and craftsmanship throughout the project pipeline, pointing to ongoing and upcoming projects such as Vogtle Units 3 and 4, and the Natrium Demonstration Project, as milestones that the partnership aims to support. NABTU President Sean McGarvey added that developing a robust apprenticeship program is essential as the industry moves toward more nuclear projects, emphasizing that a steady pipeline of job-ready craft professionals is critical to avoid shortages.

+ CHILE'S MINING PIPELINE TO BENEFIT FROM BECHTEL-EIMISA COLLABORATION

A global leader in engineering and construction has formed a strategic partnership with Echeverria Izquierdo Montajes Industriales S.A. (EIMISA), a prominent Chilean firm specializing in construction and industrial services. The announcement was made on 6th May 2026.As mining investments surge in the region, there is a growing need for efficient project execution that meets rigorous safety and quality standards. The partnership will leverage the strengths of both companies; the engineering and project management expertise of the global firm will complement EIMISA's direct-hire construction throughout Chile.Lucy Martin, President of Bechtel's Mining & Metals division, emphasized the need for a refined execution model, stating, "This partnership reflects a deliberate approach to execution, combining our end-to-end delivery capability with EIMISA's strong construction presence in Chile to strengthen how we deliver across the region."

+ BECHTEL PARTNERS WITH SOWELA TO STRENGTHEN LOCAL PIPEFITTING SKILLS PIPELINE

Bechtel and SOWELA Technical Community College are launching a no-cost pipefitting training program at SOWELA's Main Campus in Lake Charles, designed to build a local pipeline of skilled workers for manufacturing, energy, and building systems. The program runs from 26th May 2026 through 13th November 2026.The program provides an introduction to the pipefitting trade, including core responsibilities, safety principles, and the tools and materials commonly used on the job. Trainees will gain hands-on experience with high- and low-pressure piping systems that support plants, facilities, and infrastructure.Classes meet Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 3 p.m., with completion yielding an OSHA 10-hour safety credential. Participants will be prepared to pursue NCCER Pipefitting certifications, which can boost employability in a competitive local job market.To be eligible for employment consideration, completers must pass a background check and drug screening. Interested residents can learn more or register at www.sowela.edu/pipefitting, and can reach SOWELA's Office of Workforce Solutions at (337) 421-6560 or workforce@sowela.edu for questions.The Bechtel-SOWELA collaboration underscores a shared commitment to workforce development in Lake Charles, addressing rising demand for skilled labor in construction and manufacturing and supporting regional economic growth.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ KIEWIT-AUSTIN COMMERCIAL JV TO DELIVER OPERATIONS & MAINTENANCE FACILITY FOR AUSTIN LIGHT RAIL

On 28th April 2026, a joint venture between Kiewit Building Group and Austin Commercial secured a design-build contract to construct an operations and maintenance facility for the US$7.1 billion Austin Light Rail line, which will span 15 stations along a 10-mile corridor and feature all-electric trains expected to run every five to ten minutes through much of the day.Kiewit and Austin Commercial will design and build the facility where Austin’s light rail vehicles will be stored, serviced, and dispatched to ensure reliable daily service.In addition to storage and maintenance, the facility will provide workspace for operators, maintenance crews, and support staff, contributing to the overall functionality of the system.The project includes about 10 design-stage subcontractors and is expected to generate thousands of jobs during both the design and construction phases.ATP Chief Executive Greg Canally said, "This is another major step forward, and we now have the full construction team that will deliver Austin’s light rail system. The Operations and Maintenance Facility is integral to the success of the system—it’s where trains are maintained, cleaned and prepared for service each day." Canally added that partnering with Kiewit, a world-class firm with deep local experience, positions the project for long-term success and broader economic opportunities across Central Texas. The LINC Austin program, initially led by Aecom, includes Parsons, STV, Turner & Townsend, Ardmore Roderick, and CAS Consulting among its delivery-partner collaborators.

+ AUSTIN TRANSIT PARTNERSHIP NAMES KAP TO LEAD OMF FOR PROJECT CONNECT

The Austin Transit Partnership's board has chosen Kiewit Austin Partnership, a joint venture of Kiewit Building Group Inc. and Austin Commercial, as the design-build contractor for the Operations and Maintenance Facility, enabling immediate pre-construction activities such as design advancement, permitting, and site preparation. The decision was announced on 21st April 2026.The OMF footprint covers about 62 acres on the southwest edge of the Montopolis neighborhood, near U.S. 183 and SH 71, and will house storage and servicing for up to 40 light rail vehicles. To make room for the facility, property acquisitions will affect dozens of existing businesses, with relocation concerns centered on Airport Commerce Park.KAP currently brings roughly 10 subcontractors into the design phase, with more work expected as construction ramps up. The broader Project Connect program is projected to create or support thousands—potentially tens of thousands—of jobs across Texas during design and construction. The OMF is expected to support about 300 long-term jobs once the system is operational, contributing to local economic growth. Mainline construction is expected to start in 2027, with initial service targeted for the early 2030s. The Federal Transit Administration published its final Environmental Impact Statement and Record of Decision in January 2026, allowing the project to move into final design and land acquisition.

+ KIEWIT TO LEAD PRE-FEED FOR ATERNIUM'S $1 BILLION HYDROGEN AND HEAVY WATER PLANT

Aternium has appointed Kiewit Engineering Group Inc. to undertake the pre-front-end engineering and design (pre-FEED) for its first integrated hydrogen and heavy water production facility, a step in its roughly $1 billion plan to produce clean hydrogen and critical heavy water domestically. The announcement was made on 26th March 2026.The Mid-Atlantic site is designed to meet rising demand for low-carbon energy and strengthen domestic capabilities for heavy water, which is currently sourced primarily from countries such as China and Iran. Andrew Cottone, founder and CEO of Aternium, noted that the collaboration with Kiewit aligns with the company's emphasis on technical excellence and safety standards.During the pre-FEED phase, Kiewit will help define the project scope, optimize design parameters, and lay a solid foundation for future development, according to the companies. Tyler Nordquist, executive vice president at Kiewit Engineering Group Inc., said the partnership will add substantial value in this early stage.Aternium's integrated production approach combines clean hydrogen generation with a patent-pending process for isolating heavy water, a dual output designed to lower hydrogen production costs and to address vulnerabilities in the U.S. supply of strategic materials. The project sits at the intersection of energy transition and advanced manufacturing and aims to deliver scalable benefits to local communities through workforce development and job creation.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION TO GROW INNSBROOK FOOTPRINT WITH 9,100-SF EXPANSION BACKED BY HENRICO COUNTY

DPR Construction announced a 9,100-square-foot expansion of its Innsbrook office, supported by Henrico County incentives to attract and retain businesses. The date of this announcement is 29th April 2026.The expansion aims to accommodate a growing workforce and strengthen operational capabilities as part of the company’s regional growth strategy.The project will bring together teams and resources, promoting collaboration and innovation across the organization.Sustainable design features and modern office concepts will be integrated, aligning with DPR’s leadership in sustainable construction.Completion is expected within the next year, underscoring the company’s commitment to the region and its ongoing community initiatives.

+ JASON PAGE APPOINTED HEAD OF DPR CONSTRUCTION'S NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP

The Special Services Group handles a broad range of work, from targeted improvements like parking garage stair installations to expansive interior buildouts for new office and entertainment spaces, and Page's leadership is expected to expand the group's capabilities to meet evolving client and stakeholder needs in Nashville. Beyond project leadership, Page mentors and coaches teams, sharing best practices for quick-turn, high-impact work and fostering a culture of collaboration and innovation as DPR expands its Nashville footprint.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION SECURES GREAT PLACE TO WORK STATUS AS STAFF REPORT HIGH ENGAGEMENT

DPR Construction has secured Great Place To Work Certification, confirming that 97 percent of its employees say it is a great place to work, a figure about 40 points higher than the national average. The award, presented on 31st March 2026, signals a culture rooted in pride, belonging and opportunity across the firm’s projects.CEO George Pfeffer noted the certification reflects sentiments across the organization. "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life," he said. The company emphasizes trust, support, and growth to help employees perform at their best.DPR’s operating model empowers staff to make decisions and share information openly, encouraging initiative and cross functional collaboration on complex tasks, new roles, and ideas beyond job descriptions. A strong emphasis on learning and development supports clear career paths aligned with individual aspirations.Great Place To Work® research aligns with these findings, highlighting a sense of pride and belonging among workers. The certification has helped DPR strengthen its reputation as an employer of choice, while external accolades from U.S. News & World Report and Newsweek have recognized DPR in more than 15 U.S. cities over the last year.Founded in 1990, DPR has evolved into a multi billion dollar contractor known for complex and sustainable projects across sectors. Its innovation posture—through virtual design and construction and self performing craft—helps explain its roughly 11,000 person workforce worldwide. More information about DPR’s culture and career opportunities is available on its official website.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PARSONS CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ PARSONS CORPORATION (NYSE:PSN) JOINS WITH EVERYWHERE COMMUNICATIONS TO DEVELOP RESILIENT AUTONOMOUS DRONE PLATFORM

Parsons Corporation (NYSE:PSN) and EVERYWHERE Communications forged a strategic partnership to accelerate autonomous drone development under the SBIR initiative, targeting operations in degraded and disconnected environments. The partnership was confirmed on 4th of May 2026. The collaboration leverages Iridium Satellite technology to deliver a resilient data transport layer that lets drones operate autonomously while streaming essential sensor data back to operators and command systems. The system supports beyond-line-of-sight operations, reliable data exfiltration and autonomous mission execution, with scalable coordination for fleets of drones on low-bandwidth links. Jake Bailey, President of EVERYWHERE Communications, notes, "In austere environments, connectivity is never guaranteed. We're enabling drones to keep operating—and keep delivering intelligence—even when the network is compromised." Parsons contributes TAK-as-a-Service (TaaS) to provide scalable, mission-ready integration and sustainment services tailored for the Department of War and federal agencies, enhancing real-time situational awareness and data interoperability. Mike Kushin, President of Defense and Intelligence at Parsons, adds, "In an era where every second and every signal matters, this collaboration brings together resilient autonomy in the air and trusted mission systems on the ground to give our warfighters and intelligence professionals a decisive information advantage." The alliance seeks to redefine operational capabilities for mission-critical teams worldwide.

+ PARSONS CORPORATION (NYSE: PSN) SETS DELEGATED PROGRAM MANAGER ROLE FOR THE BORING COMPANY'S DUBAI LOOP

CHANTILLY, Va. — The Boring Company awards Parsons a nine-month contract for the Dubai Loop project, dated 5th May 2026Parsons will serve as TBC’s delegated program manager, overseeing design-build activities for the Dubai Loop project and delivering Independent Design Verification, Stakeholder Management, and Permitting and NOC Support for the pilot phase. The firm will also perform multidisciplinary reviews of designs across civil, structural, mechanical, electrical, safety, and utility disciplinesPierre Santoni, president of Infrastructure EMEA at Parsons, highlighted the UAE's ongoing infrastructure portfolio and stated, "Parsons has proudly played a central role in developing some of the most important and prestigious critical infrastructure programs across the UAE since our first programs there in the 1980s." He added that the partnership with TBC aligns with the UAE leadership's vision to foster strategic partnerships and enhance advanced technologies as part of the Dubai 2040 Urban Master PlanThe Dubai Loop project, developed in collaboration with Dubai’s Roads and Transit Authority (RTA), aims to expand the city’s transport ecosystem through a series of passenger transport tunnels and stations. Parsons has a history of working with the RTA, having contributed to significant transportation infrastructure projects in the Emirate since 2005, including the Dubai Metro and various highway and bridge projectsParsons Corporation has managed over 250 complex tunneling projects globally and has extensive experience in road and highway development, covering more than 10,000 miles across six continents. As a provider of disruptive technologies in national security and global infrastructure, Parsons is well-positioned to contribute to the Dubai Loop initiative, enhancing urban mobility and transportation efficiency in one of the world's most dynamic cities

+ PARSONS CORPORATION (NYSE:PSN) WINS NYC DEP PROJECT FOR LARGE-DIAMETER STORAGE TUNNEL AND DEWATERING PUMP STATION AND SECURES USCG PROGRAM MANAGEMENT CONTRACT VIA GSI AMERICAS JV

Parsons Corporation (NYSE:PSN) Posts Progress On Apr. 14, 2026 Contract Award To Lead The Construction Of The Newtown Creek CSO Storage Tunnel In New York City. The Project Features A Large-Diameter Storage Tunnel And A Dewatering Pump Station To Curb Untreated DischargesThe New York City Department Of Environmental Protection Has Entrusted Parsons With This Critical Task, Citing The Firm's Experience On More Than 250 Tunnel And Ocean Outfall Projects Worldwide. The Initiative Aims To Modernize Municipal Assets, Improve Water Quality, And Support Urban Sustainability Through A Technology-Driven ApproachIn Addition, Parsons Forms A Joint Venture With GSI Americas That Secures A US Coast Guard Program Management Contract Worth $25 Million. The Contract Supports The USCG Execution Plan, Including Significant Upgrades To Shore Infrastructure And Deployment Of Transformational TechnologiesThe Partnership Highlights Parsons' Strategic Intent To Assist Federal Agencies In Securing And Modernizing Critical Digital And Physical Assets. The Arrangement Aligns With The Company's Broader Focus On Defense And Cybersecurity Markets And Reflects Its Ability To Deliver Integrated Solutions For Urban And National Security ChallengesInvestors May Monitor The Company's Ongoing Growth Across Infrastructure And Defense Initiatives As Parsons Expands Its Portfolio
